Implicaties - Logging van interacties: Input, output en relevante context van AI-interacties worden vastgelegd. - Model- en policyversiebeheer: Exacte versies van modellen, prompts en policies worden geregistreerd. - Provenance-metadata: Vastlegging van herkomst van data en context (bijv. welke bron in RAG is gebruikt). - Prompt- en contextregistratie: Gebruikte prompts en contextselecties worden opgeslagen voor analyse en reproductie. - End-to-end traceability: Complete keten van input tot output is traceerbaar, ook bij multi-step workflows. - Audit trails: Vastlegging van wie wat heeft gedaan, wanneer en onder welke voorwaarden. - Reproduceerbaarheid: Het moet mogelijk zijn om een AI-uitkomst opnieuw te genereren met dezelfde inputs en configuratie.

element.name: AI Platform Architecture Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to define, maintain, and govern the architecture of the AI Platform across its current (as-is) and target (to-be) states, using architectural principles, standards, and models to guide the platform’s evolution in alignment with enterprise strategy, regulatory requirements, and ethical constraints.
This capability produces and manages architectural blueprints, reference architectures, and transition roadmaps for the AI Platform, and uses these artifacts to steer strategic investment decisions, ensure architectural coherence, and enable controlled and sustainable platform growth.
The purpose of the AI Platform Architecture Management Capability is to: - Steer strategic decisions related to the positioning, scope, and evolution of the AI Platform - Ensure architectural coherence across platform components (data, knowledge, AI, governance, infrastructure) - Anticipate and respond holistically to external drivers such as regulation (e.g. AI Act), technological change, and enterprise strategy - Provide decision-makers with clear architectural options and trade-offs for evolving the platform

element.name: Volledige Traceerbaarheid
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Bron, verwerking, context, versies en gebruik van data moeten altijd herleidbaar zijn.
Rationale Essentieel voor auditability, explainability en naleving van AI Act en AVG.
Consequenties Auditlogs voor alle bewerkingen en beslissingen. Provenance-metadata verplicht. Toegangsbeleid voor auditinformatie (functioneel beheer/compliance).

element.name: Geen Retentie bij Externe LLM’s
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Externe modelproviders mogen ingestuurde data niet bewaren of hergebruiken.
Rationale Beschermt vertrouwelijke klantdata tegen ongewenste training of opslag.
Consequenties Providerkeuze afhankelijk van retentiebeleid. Instellen van API-parameters (no logging / no data retention). Due diligence / DPIA vereist.

element.name: AI Solution Monitoring & Behavioral Oversight Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to continuously monitor the runtime behavior, performance characteristics, policy compliance, and operational signals of active AI Solutions, detecting anomalies, drift, misuse, or governance deviations.
This capability provides structured oversight without intervening in business logic or assuming outcome ownership.
Purpose - Detect abnormal behavior or degradation - Surface policy violations or misuse - Provide early warning signals for risk escalation - Enable responsible oversight of active AI Solutions

element.name: Security Specialist
element.documentation: “Security Vulnerability Management … prompt injection, data poisoning, model inversion … mitigation requires a layered security posture.” (p9)

element.name: AI Platform Compliance Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to ensure that the AI Platform is designed, operated, and evolved in compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal governance frameworks by translating compliance obligations into platform-level requirements, controls, and evidence.
This capability supports continuous compliance through monitoring, documentation, and assurance rather than periodic, point-in-time assessments.

element.name: Klant Data-Eigenaarschap
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Klant blijft eigenaar van alle data en bepaalt waar en hoe deze wordt opgeslagen en verwerkt.
Rationale Borgt vertrouwen, juridische compliance (AVG), en controle over datastromen, zeker bij hybride/SaaS use cases.
Consequenties Klant bepaalt bewaartermijnen, residencies en toegang. Leverancier garandeert verwerking binnen overeengekomen regio’s/omgevingen. DPA (verwerkersovereenkomst) verplicht. Tenant-isolatie en encryptie zijn vereist.

element.name: AI Platform Deployment Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to provision, configure, and activate access to AI Platform capabilities and assets in response to approved requests, ensuring secure, policy-compliant, and traceable enablement of platform services.
Deployment primarily concerns the activation of platform services, access rights, configurations, and governance controls rather than the deployment of AI solutions.
What is AI-platform specific: - Deployment often means: - granting access to data or knowledge assets - enabling APIs or platform services - configuring policy enforcement and monitoring - Emphasis on: - least-privilege access - traceable provisioning - reproducibility and rollback

element.name: Data Protection & Privacy
element.documentation: Detects and redacts PII/PHI, applies tokenization or masking, and performs data loss prevention for prompts, retrieved context, and generated outputs.

element.name: Policy Telemetry Stream
element.documentation: Aggregated stream/queue topic of decision + enforcement events (used for near-real-time monitoring).

element.name: Resource
element.documentation:
<<<
*Purpose*
The ((Resource)) functional component manages the pool of resources (a logical abstraction of digital assets) that support Desired Product Instances delivering digital services, such as infrastructure, applications, and third-party services. This also ensures that the resource requirements for the services and their underlying resources are well understood and provisioned efficiently.
The Resource functional component ensures the pooling of resources that may be virtual or physical (such as server, storage, networking, software licenses, IP address, etc.) are made available for the Order fulfillment. It maintains the soft limits or hard limits of resource pooling for digital resources(e.g., cloud resources) and acts appropriately when there is a breach of the limits. It also provides resource utilization details for Usage functional components to compute costs.
*Functional Criteria*
The Resource functional component:
* Shall manage the pool of Resources that support services, such as infrastructure, applications, and third-party services * Shall ensure that the digital Resource requirements against the services are ascertained * Shall ensure the availability of physical assets/virtual Resource pools for the Order fulfillment * Shall maintain the soft limits, hard limits, quotas, etc. of Resource pools for digital resources (e.g., cloud resources), and act appropriately when there is a breach of the limits * May provide capacity Resource utilization details to the Usage functional component for cost computation

element.name: AI Platform Risk Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to identify, assess, monitor, and mitigate risks associated with the AI Platform, including technical, operational, ethical, legal, and societal risks that may emerge from platform capabilities or usage.
This capability addresses both known and emergent risks and supports timely intervention, restriction, or adjustment of platform behavior when risk thresholds are exceeded.

element.name: AI Solution Qualification & Design Framing Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to support structured intake and early-stage shaping of proposed AI Solutions by providing templates, evaluation criteria, feasibility indicators, and preliminary governance signals.
This capability helps solution owners clarify objectives, scope, dependencies, and platform implications before formal portfolio evaluation, without determining business value or ownership.
Purpose - Improve clarity and maturity of AI Solution proposals - Identify feasibility constraints and platform dependencies early - Surface preliminary risk and governance considerations - Reduce late-stage redesign and approval friction - Enable consistent intake practices across use cases

element.name: AI Solution Configuration & Policy Enforcement Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to configure and enforce solution-specific policies, operational parameters, quotas, monitoring rules, and governance controls when AI Solutions are activated for use.
This capability ensures that usage constraints defined during design and approval are technically enforced at runtime.
Purpose - Translate governance decisions into runtime controls - Prevent divergence between approved scope and actual usage - Enable controlled scaling of solution consumption - Preserve compliance posture during operation

element.name: Define Backlog Mandates
element.documentation: The purpose of the value stream stage "`Define Backlog Mandates`" is to determine if there are any mandates or directives that will impact the Product Portfolio, and to ensure mandates are clearly defined in backlog items and related in Scope Agreements. This should include a review of the existing portfolio and managed service provider agreements. Portfolio rationalization to balance the portfolio should be completed regularly and may generate backlog items that are bundled into Scope Agreements. Upon approval to proceed on a Scope Agreement, initial funding is allocated to the Explore value stream to identify concerns specific to a Digital Product and determine feasibility. The deliverables for this value stream stage are refined Scope Agreements to reflect mandates and defined Architecture Roadmap Items.

element.name: Knowledge Sharing & Skills Development Process
element.documentation: Plan and execute AI-related training, mentoring, and workshops. Capture and disseminate lessons learned and best practices across AI teams.

element.name: Refine Product Backlog
element.documentation:
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Refine Product Backlog`" is to translate a Scope Agreement into smaller pieces of work that can be assigned to product level features and linked to the product backlog. This includes organizing assigned backlog items into work packages and adjusting the Scope Agreement, business case, and budget as needed. The deliverables for this value stream stage are established Architecture Roadmap Items.
<<<

element.name: API Gateway
element.documentation: “Workflow/function-based or agentic frameworks and communication protocols.” → Implies API and message routing infrastructure. p. 4 (“GenAI Tech Stack Architecture” diagram description)

element.name: Neem gegevens als fundament
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Gebruik gegevens als fundament voor het ontwerp, realisatie en doorontwikkeling van de dienst en richt het beheer hiervan goed in.
Rationale De kwaliteit en toegankelijkheid van gegevens bepaalt de waarde van de dienst.
De onderliggende gegevens kennen meestal een veel langere levenscyclus dan de systemen waarin deze verwerkt worden. Als gegevens op duurzame wijze toegankelijk zijn gemaakt voor mens én machine kunnen deze gegevens dienen als fundering voor verschillende diensten in verschillende toepassingen, door de tijd en over de hele keten heen.
Daarom is het essentieel dat het beheer van gegevens in de keten op orde is, vanaf het ontstaan ervan tot en met de vernietiging (conform bewaartermijnen).
Realiseert doel(-en) Begrijpelijk Beschikbaar Betrouwbaar Gebundeld Integer Kostenefficiënt Overzichtelijk Toegankelijk Uniform Vindbaar
Alle implicaties bij dit architectuurprincipe IMP001 Beschrijf informatieobjecten in een model IMP021 Bevorder hergebruik van gegevens IMP049 Draag zorg voor juiste en actuele en volledige informatie IMP044 Hanteer bewaartermijnen voor informatie IMP051 Leg auditlogs vast bij de bronregistratie van het gegeven IMP048 Leg de context van een informatieobject vast in metadata IMP050 Maak gegevens herleidbaar tot de bron (herkomst) IMP003 Maak zoveel mogelijk data beschikbaar als open data IMP047 Pas de FAIR dataprincipes toe IMP052 Sla informatie op in een duurzaam toegankelijk bestandsformaat IMP073 Stel van ieder gegeven de kwaliteit vast IMP058 Stel voor ieder gegeven de unieke bron vast IMP046 Stel één verantwoordelijke vast voor ieder gegeven IMP002 Voorkom verlies van informatie

element.name: Beheersing van context en kennisgebruik
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Het platform beheerst welke kennis, bronnen en context gebruikt mogen worden door AI-solutions en onder welke voorwaarden.
Rationale De kwaliteit, betrouwbaarheid en veiligheid van AI-uitkomsten zijn direct afhankelijk van de gebruikte context en kennisbronnen. Zonder expliciete beheersing bestaat het risico op gebruik van onjuiste, verouderde of ongeautoriseerde informatie, wat kan leiden tot verkeerde beslissingen of datalekken. Door contextgebruik te sturen wordt consistentie, betrouwbaarheid en compliance geborgd.
Implicaties - Whitelisting van bronnen: Alleen goedgekeurde databronnen mogen gebruikt worden voor AI-context. - Contextselectie per use case: Per toepassing wordt expliciet bepaald welke kennis relevant en toegestaan is. - Scheiding vertrouwelijke en publieke data: Data wordt geclassificeerd en alleen gebruikt binnen toegestane contexten. - RAG governance: Beheer van welke documenten, datasets en kennisbanken worden ingezet. - Geen vrije contextinjectie: Developers en gebruikers kunnen niet willekeurig context toevoegen buiten gecontroleerde mechanismen. - Metadata en classificatie van bronnen: Bronnen worden voorzien van metadata (bijv. betrouwbaarheid, actualiteit). - Versiebeheer van kennis: Wijzigingen in kennisbronnen zijn traceerbaar en beheersbaar. - Validatie van contextgebruik: Gebruik van context wordt gecontroleerd op compliance en relevantie.

element.name: Asset Management
element.documentation:
The ability to identify, register, track, and manage AI Platform assets throughout their lifecycle, ensuring visibility, ownership, and controlled use. Assets include platform components, knowledge products, configurations, and governance artifacts required for platform operation and accountability.
Assets are managed primarily to support accountability, compliance, reproducibility, and controlled reuse of platform capabilities.

element.name: AI Knowledge Asset Management & Delivery Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to enable AI Solution teams to onboard, curate, version, and safely apply governed knowledge assets (documents, datasets, ontologies/knowledge graphs, embeddings, and retrieval configurations) throughout the AI Solution realization process, ensuring that solutions can be grounded in approved sources and evolve knowledge safely without ad-hoc or uncontrolled content usage.
This capability also supports controlled use of less-governed or exploratory sources in designated contexts (e.g., sandbox or research modes), with explicit labeling, risk-based restrictions, and traceability safeguards.
This capability provides structured knowledge scaffolding and delivery mechanisms while leaving substantive accountability for knowledge selection, interpretation, and use with the AI Solution owner.
Purpose - Enable consistent, reusable use of domain knowledge in AI Solutions - Prevent uncontrolled or non-approved source usage and “hidden” knowledge dependencies - Ensure provenance, traceability, and explainability via grounded sources and versioned knowledge assets - Reduce operational risk from stale, drifting, or inconsistent knowledge inputs - Enable safe exploration by allowing less-governed sources under controlled modes and explicit risk posture

element.name: AI Platform Performance Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to monitor and manage the performance of AI Platform services and pipelines, including responsiveness, throughput, consistency, and behavioral characteristics that affect reliability and trust.
Performance management considers both technical metrics and platform-specific indicators such as response stability, retrieval quality, and reproducibility under load.

element.name: Policy Obligations / Constraints
element.documentation:
Policy Decision ≠ simple allow/deny
A modern PDP typically returns: - Decision: Permit / Deny - Obligations: things the platform MUST do - Constraints: limitations under which access is allowed
Examples of Policy Obligations - Log the request for audit - Mask or redact PII fields - Enforce human-in-the-loop - Store prompts and responses - Attach purpose metadata
Examples of Policy Constraints - Use only approved model(s) - Limit output length - Apply rate limits - Restrict to EU region - Disable external API calls

element.name: AI Platform Product Design Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to design the logical and physical structure of AI Platform services and data/knowledge components, translating requirements and architectural principles into concrete platform designs that can be implemented and governed.
This capability defines how platform services, pipelines, and governance mechanisms are composed, interact, and expose controlled interfaces to consumers.
What is AI-platform specific: - Design spans: - knowledge models and metadata - AI service interfaces - governance and oversight hooks - Emphasis on: - reuse - composability - enforceability of policy by design

element.name: Hosted LLM Provider
element.documentation: “Managed services like OpenAI’s GPT, Azure OpenAI, or Anthropic’s Claude provide ease of use but may introduce vendor lock-in.” p. 5

element.name: Ingestion & Preprocessing Pipeline
element.documentation: “Data and Data Pipeline – Mission-specific data used for steering or training models … data pipelines must address preprocessing, transformation, integration.” p. 4 and p. 6 (Data and Pipeline Layer)

element.name: AI Integration & Deployment Process
element.documentation: Package and deploy AI models into production environments. Manage integration with applications and APIs, ensuring automation and traceability through version control and CI/CD pipelines.

element.name: AI Demand & Capacity Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to understand, shape, prioritize, and balance demand for AI platform capabilities—such as data, models, knowledge assets, compute capacity, experimentation environments, and governance support—against available technical, organizational, and ethical capacity.
This capability distinguishes between exploratory and production-grade demand, manages capacity constraints (e.g. compute, data readiness, governance throughput), and supports strategic decision-making on scaling, throttling, or gating access to AI platform services.
Key AI-specific aspects - Differentiation between experimental, pilot, and regulated production demand - Capacity management across compute, data quality, and governance review capabilities - Demand steering through prioritization, quotas, and access controls aligned with strategic themes

element.name: AI Testing & Validation Support Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide testing environments, validation tooling, evaluation metrics, and reproducibility mechanisms that enable AI Solution owners to verify functional correctness, performance characteristics, and governance compliance prior to deployment.
This capability supports structured validation without certifying business outcomes.
Purpose - Enable consistent validation practices - Support reproducibility and explainability - Surface performance and governance issues early - Reduce operational instability

element.name: Avoid vendor-lockin
element.documentation: This can be achieved by introducing an orchestration layer that choses which LLM model is used. These could be of different providers.

element.name: Tenant-Isolatie en Residency
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Data wordt gescheiden per klant-tenants en binnen overeengekomen regio’s verwerkt.
Rationale Bevordert veiligheid en naleving van wetgeving (privacy, AI Act).
Consequenties Technische scheiding in opslag en compute. Gecontroleerde datalocatie in EU/NL. Logging en audit per tenant.

element.name: Design & Develop
element.documentation:
<<< *Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Design & Develop`" is to analyze the requirements, create (or update) the design artifacts, and develop the changes (and associated Test Cases) for the Product Release. This includes the coding and configuration of software and related infrastructure (e.g., infrastructure as code).
This value stream stage covers the development of the changes that are part of the new release of the Digital Product based on the requirements, architecture boundaries, and policies set by the organization. In addition, requests might be needed to cater for setting up a development environment for the developers.

element.name: Policy Drift & Anomaly Detector
element.documentation: Detects drift between desired policy state and observed enforcement reality; flags anomalies (unexpected allows, bypass patterns).

element.name: Responsible AI Governance Process
element.documentation:
Define ethical guidelines and accountability structures for AI systems. Review project proposals, monitor compliance, and coordinate periodic audits and certifications.
GRC section on ethics “Responsible AI and Ethical Guardrails … Transparency, Explainability, Accountability …” p. 9

element.name: Defect Management
element.documentation:
The ability to keep track of all Defects by registering Defects of all types (including security-related Defects). It analyzes Defects and finds resolutions. It also associates Defects with Requirements.
It documents issues that should be communicated to the Release Composition functional component. It decides on target release and reports Defect status. It also converts Defects not resolved to Known Errors for Problem Management.

element.name: Operations Management
element.documentation: The ability to perform activities carried out by IT Operations Control, including Console Management, Job Scheduling, Backup and Restore, and Print and Output Management. IT Operations is also used as a synonym for Service Operation

element.name: Response Generation
element.documentation: The LLM generates an answer together with source citations.

element.name: AI Platform Knowledge & Retrieval Policy Enforcement
element.documentation:
Enforces policies on data access and retrieval across ingestion and retrieval processes. Controls which sources and collections may be used, how sensitivity labels and classifications are applied, which retrieval strategies are allowed, and which provenance and traceability requirements must be met. Prevents unauthorized or overly sensitive information from being retrieved or returned as context. Integrates policy checks into query planning, filtering, and context assembly.
aka: PEP (Knowledge/RAG)

element.name: Support & Enablement Process
element.documentation: Offer technical and functional support to AI service users. Manage incidents, change requests, and provide enablement materials and workshops.

element.name: Policy Feedback Request
element.documentation: Actionable item sent back to policy lifecycle: change request, tuning suggestion, new rule requirement, rollback request.

element.name: Diagnose Issue
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Diagnose Issue`" is to prepare the best/quickest course of action to retain or restore normal operations. Further diagnosis can be performed to identify the root cause(s) of a potential or detected issue.

element.name: Model Security Assessment Process
element.documentation: Evaluate AI models before deployment for robustness, privacy, and exposure to adversarial or leakage risks. Document findings and enforce remediation actions.

element.name: Dataminimalisatie
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Alleen de minimaal noodzakelijke data wordt verwerkt of gedeeld binnen een taak of sessie.
Rationale Voorkomt overmatige blootstelling van gegevens en minimaliseert risico’s bij verwerking door LLM’s.
Consequenties Klant specificeert documenttypen en gevoeligheidsniveaus per use case. Leverancier past contextfiltering en chunking toe. Alleen bits die nodig zijn gaan naar modellen (no full payload).

element.name: GenAI Web App
element.documentation: “UI/Application – End-user software applications, including web interfaces, desktop applications, mobile apps … through which users access and use the LLM.” p. 4 (GenAI Tech Stack figure + UI/Application description)

element.name: AI Governance & Compliance Service
element.documentation: “Establishing clear GRC practices … fundamental to building and maintaining trust … ensuring systems are secure, ethically aligned, and compliant.” p. 9

element.name: AI Platform Product & Service Portfolio Management
element.documentation:
The ability to define, maintain, and govern the portfolio of AI Platform services and knowledge products across their full lifecycle—from concept and introduction to evolution, deprecation, and retirement—aimed at maximizing platform coherence, reuse, compliance, and strategic value.
This capability maintains the authoritative view of what the AI Platform offers (and has offered), under which conditions, and with which guarantees, independently of the AI solutions that consume those offerings.

element.name: AI Platform Technical Operations Administration Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to install, configure, patch, maintain, and technically administer platform software, middleware, runtimes, container platforms, and supporting operational components required to keep the platform functional, secure, and maintainable.
This capability ensures that the platform remains operable and technically healthy over time, beyond initial deployment, through disciplined technical administration and lifecycle management of its foundational components.
Purpose - Ensure ongoing technical administration of platform components - Maintain platform health through patching, upgrades, configuration, and housekeeping - Reduce operational degradation caused by unmanaged technical dependencies - Support maintainability and controlled evolution of the platform foundation

element.name: Workforce Management
element.documentation: In a digital ecosystem it is important to support employees in their career, providing coaching and training, as well ensuring the resource portfolio, skills, and competencies are aligned with the strategic direction. This function defines the functional components and data objects which need to be integrated with the digital delivery model.

element.name: Model Development Process
element.documentation:
Explore and design new AI models and algorithms. Conduct experimentation, parameter tuning, and evaluation using curated datasets in a controlled environment.
“Develop, train, and deploy AI models.” p. 5

element.name: Model Monitoring & Evaluation Process
element.documentation:
Continuously monitor the performance and behavior of deployed AI and LLM systems. Detect drift, data anomalies, or accuracy degradation and trigger retraining as needed.
LLMOps functions “Tracking key metrics … evaluating relevance, coherence, factual accuracy … Feedback loops and retraining strategy.” p. 8

element.name: Continuity Management
element.documentation: The ability to manage improvements to IT Service Management Processes and IT Services. The Performance of the IT Service Provider is continually measured and improvements are made to Processes, IT Services and IT Infrastructure in order to increase Efficiency, Effectiveness, and Cost Effectiveness.

element.name: Strategic Objective
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Strategic Objective)) data object defines specific actionable goals and measurable outcomes for identified stakeholders and should be related to/or provide key results for the business case.
<<<
*Key Attributes*
The Strategic Objective data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier; e.g., number of the Strategic Objective * *Name:* descriptive name of the Strategic Objective * *Description:* description/details of the Strategic Objective
*Key Data Object Relationships*
* Strategic Objective to Strategic Theme (n:m): a Strategic Objective defines details of Strategic Themes; in some instances a given objective will deliver on several themes * Strategic Objective to Portfolio Backlog Item (n:m): a Portfolio Backlog Item can be part of delivering a Strategic Objective and thus this relation can indicate the importance of a Portfolio Backlog Item as well as indicating how an objective is to be delivered * Strategic Objectives to Architecture Roadmap Item (n:m): define what activities will deliver a given objective

element.name: Respect voor menselijke autonomie
element.documentation:
Implicaties - AI en algoritmen moeten worden ontworpen en ingezet op een manier die het recht van het individu respecteert om zijn eigen beslissingen te nemen en controle te behouden over zijn handelingen. - AI en algoritmen moeten individuen in staat stellen om hun besluitvormingsvermogen te vergroten, in plaats van hen onevenredig te beïnvloeden of te manipuleren. - Betekenisvolle menselijke tussenkomst is een feit.
Bron: Governancekader Verantwoorde inzet algoritmen en AI

element.name: Multimodal Generation Service
element.documentation:
“LLMs are the core component for most enterprise GenAI applications due to their reasoning abilities and capacity to generate human-like language.” p. 6 (LLM Layer)
combines or can handle more than one modality — e.g., text + image, text + audio, image → text, etc.

element.name: Review Process (HITL)
element.documentation:
Provide human review and approval in AI workflows. Escalate edge cases or sensitive outcomes for manual validation to ensure trust and accountability in AI-supported decisions.
Human-in-the-loop control points “Human-in-the-loop … positioning humans as valuable guides when agents encounter limitations.” p. 7

element.name: AI Platform Service Level Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to define, monitor, and manage service level objectives for AI Platform capabilities, reflecting not only availability and performance but also quality, reliability, and governance commitments.
Service levels are expressed as platform guarantees and expectations rather than business outcome SLAs for individual AI solutions.

element.name: AI Platform Edge Policy Enforcement
element.documentation:
Enforces policies at the platform boundary before requests enter internal runtimes. Applies authentication integration, authorization checks, tenant routing constraints, rate limits and quotas, and basic request validation. Invokes the Policy Decision function where needed and records enforcement events for monitoring and audit. Reduces blast radius by blocking non-compliant traffic as early as possible.
aka: PEP (Edge)

element.name: Object Storage Service
element.documentation: “Infrastructure refers to the physical or cloud-based resources that power data storage, processing, and AI computations.” p. 5

element.name: Portfolio Backlog Management
element.documentation: Management of the Portfolio Backlog for the lifecycle management and visibility of the Portfolio Backlog Items as grouped into collections of work and prioritized.

element.name: Handling outcome
element.documentation: Handling outcome is derived from authorization and policy decisions.

element.name: Architecture Management
element.documentation: The ability to manage architecture is required to effectively describe the as-is and target architecture or 'blueprints' of the enterprise, to define the policies and standards used by it, and govern solution compliance to the defined architecture, policies, and standards. At an enterprise level, it includes enterprise architecture, the sub-domain specialties of enterprise architecture; at a tactical or service level, it includes solution architecture, service architecture, and systems (technology) architecture. The purpose of the Manage Architecture Capability is to use architecture policies and artifacts to provide the organization: - The ability to steer strategic decisions toward wanted positions and business outcomes (the future state) - The ability to proactively and holistically lead enterprise responses to business strategies and external drivers. - The ability to present business and IT leaders with recommendations to achieve target business outcomes and secure implementation of the transition roadmap.

element.name: Prompt Enrichment & Routing Component
element.documentation:
“In multi-model applications, an orchestration layer analyzes incoming requests and routes them to the most appropriate model for the task.” p. 6
Clarifies its scope: builds/enriches prompts and decides routing, no orchestration logic.

element.name: Authorization is not isolation
element.documentation:
Tenant isolation is enforced by architecture, not by policy alone. Policy (PAP/PDP/PEP) governs whether actions are permitted, but isolation mechanisms ensure permitted actions cannot cross tenant boundaries—even in the presence of bugs, misconfiguration, or partial compromise.
Rationale Authorization controls intent (“may this action happen?”). Isolation controls effect (“what can the action reach?”). Relying solely on authorization assumes perfect enforcement everywhere, which is unrealistic at scale.
Implications Every platform capability that touches data must enforce tenant scoping structurally (e.g., tenant-partitioned storage/indexes, row-level security, tenant-scoped credentials). Policy checks must not be treated as the only safeguard against cross-tenant access. Tenant identity should be derived from trusted context (JWT claims/service identity) and propagated end-to-end.
Anti-pattern “Tenant isolation is achieved by checking tenant_id in application code/policies.” This is fragile and prone to bypass via missed checks, new code paths, or downstream services.

element.name: AI Solution Entitlement & Access Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to manage and enforce fine-grained access rights and usage conditions for AI Solutions, based on roles, purpose, data sensitivity, and governance classification.
This capability ensures that access is conditional, auditable, and aligned with platform policy enforcement.
Purpose - Enforce least-privilege access principles - Align usage context with governance requirements - Prevent misuse or unintended expansion of solution scope - Maintain auditability of entitlements

element.name: Propose Investments
element.documentation: The purpose of the value stream stage "`Propose Investments`" is to prioritize a list of opportunities and associated Scope Agreements. The prioritization should be based on a scoring method that considers business value, risk, costs, time, and resource availability. Scope Agreements are evaluated to determine if there is already a solution in the Product Portfolio that can be used or modified to satisfy the business need. If it is determined that there is not an existing solution and the new demand is in alignment with the digital strategy, the Scope Agreement is assessed against other work in the Portfolio Backlog and prioritized accordingly. The prioritization criteria should also consider factors like urgency and the impact of opportunities. The deliverable for this value stream stage is updated Scope Agreements with clarifications discovered during a prioritization of opportunities.

element.name: Secure Remote Access / Bastion
element.documentation: Manages privileged access for administrators or CI/CD systems to GPU clusters or nodes.

element.name: Request Management
element.documentation: The ability to provide a channel for users to request and receive offered services.

element.name: Policy Compliance Evidence Service
element.documentation: Curates evidence packages, retention/immutability, produces audit exports.

element.name: AI Platform Resilience & Recovery Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to safeguard continuity of service through backup, restore, recovery, failover, and resilience measures for platform data, services, configurations, and supporting components.
This capability ensures that disruptions, corruption, or loss of critical assets can be contained and recovered from in a controlled and timely manner, preserving trust in the platform as an operational foundation.
Purpose - Ensure recoverability of platform services, data, and configurations - Minimize impact of outages, corruption, or operational failures - Support continuity through backup, restore, and resilience measures - Improve platform trustworthiness in the face of disruptions

element.name: AI Platform Configuration Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to identify, control, and maintain accurate information about the configuration of AI Platform components, including services, data pipelines, knowledge assets, policies, and operational parameters.
Configuration management supports reproducibility, traceability, and controlled change by ensuring that platform behavior can be understood and reconstructed when needed.

element.name: Duidelijke Verantwoordelijkheden
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Rollen en aansprakelijkheid (controller, processor, sub-processor) moeten expliciet zijn.
Rationale AI-ketens omvatten meerdere partijen; juridische helderheid voorkomt risico’s.
Consequenties Contractueel vastleggen van verantwoordelijkheden. Incident- en escalatieprocedures opstellen. Auditsporen per partij behouden.

element.name: Enforcement Event
element.documentation: What the PEP actually did (blocked, allowed, redacted, rate-limited, header injected, etc.), including outcome/status.

element.name: Product Design Management
element.documentation: The ability to design new or changed service software functionality and data. Ability to identify the new or existing services required to meet the needs of the Scope Agreement and IT Initiative, including both service systems and service offers.

element.name: AI Platform Contract Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to manage contracts related to the AI Platform, including agreements with data providers, AI technology vendors, cloud service providers, and external assurance parties, ensuring that contractual terms align with platform architecture, policies, and compliance requirements.
This capability addresses obligations related to data rights, model usage, auditability, and exit conditions.

element.name: Human Oversight Service
element.documentation: “Human-in-the-loop approach … valuable guides.” p. 7

element.name: AI Platform Incident Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to detect, log, assess, and resolve incidents that disrupt or threaten the correct operation, trustworthiness, or compliance of the AI Platform.
Incidents may include technical failures, data integrity breaches, governance violations, or detection of harmful or unintended platform behavior requiring immediate intervention.

element.name: Testing & Validation Process
element.documentation:
Perform structured validation of AI models before production deployment. Assess accuracy, robustness, fairness, and reproducibility to ensure compliance with quality standards.
Govern and execute testing frameworks that ensure adherence to defined quality, security, and performance criteria during model development.
Validation section “Testing must go beyond standard QA … Functional correctness, performance, robustness, reliability.” p. 9–10

element.name: AI Change Control Process
element.documentation: Register, assess, and approve proposed changes to AI systems. Maintain documentation, audit trails, and authorization before changes are implemented.

element.name: AI Platform Event Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to detect, filter, correlate, and respond to events generated by the AI Platform, including technical, data-related, behavioral, and governance-relevant signals that indicate changes in platform state or risk.
Events may originate from infrastructure, data pipelines, AI services, policy enforcement mechanisms, or monitoring of anomalous or unintended behavior.

element.name: Beleid afdwingen door Architectuur
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Beleid wordt technisch afgedwongen door het platform en niet uitsluitend door procesafspraken.
Rationale Procesafspraken en handmatige controles zijn onvoldoende schaalbaar en foutgevoelig, zeker bij dynamische AI-toepassingen. Door beleid technisch af te dwingen in de architectuur wordt naleving consistent, controleerbaar en automatisch geborgd, onafhankelijk van individuele teams of implementaties.
Implicaties - Policy-as-code: Beleidsregels worden formeel vastgelegd en versioneerbaar beheerd, zodat ze reproduceerbaar en wijzigbaar zijn. - Development-validatie: In CI/CD pipelines wordt gecontroleerd of oplossingen voldoen aan beleid (bijv. toegestane modellen, datagebruik, security policies). - Runtime-validatie: Elke request (API-call, modelaanroep, contextgebruik) wordt realtime getoetst aan policies. - Admission controls: Nieuwe workloads, modellen of integraties worden alleen toegestaan als ze voldoen aan vooraf gedefinieerde beleidsregels. - Policy decision logging: Beslissingen (toegestaan/geweigerd) worden gelogd met onderbouwing, zodat audits mogelijk zijn.

element.name: Security Platform
element.documentation: “Mitigation requires a layered security posture … input validation and sanitization, output filtering, stringent access controls, continuous monitoring for anomalous activity.” p. 9 (Security Vulnerability Management)

element.name: Policy Compliance Evidence
element.documentation: Curated evidence set for audit (immutable, time-stamped): sampled decision traces, control attestations, reports.

element.name: AI Platform Measurement & Reporting Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to define, collect, analyze, and report measurements related to the health, adoption, effectiveness, and trustworthiness of the AI Platform, providing stakeholders with insight into platform performance, risks, and governance outcomes.
This capability focuses on platform-level indicators rather than business outcomes of individual AI solutions.

element.name: Configuration & Artifact Repository
element.documentation:
A shared IT platform capability that stores, versions, and publishes deployable configuration and release artifacts used by delivery automation and runtime platforms. It acts as a system of record for “what should be deployed/run” and supports controlled promotion across environments.
It typically manages items such as: - Configuration as Code: Kubernetes manifests, Helm charts, Kustomize overlays, environment/tenant configs - Policy as Code: OPA/Rego policies, policy bundles, gateway config, guardrail rules - Release artifacts: container images, packages, charts, signed bundles - Versioning & traceability: history, tags/releases, approvals via PR/MR workflow, rollback to known-good versions - Integrity controls (optional): signatures, provenance/attestation (e.g., SLSA-style), immutable artifacts
Example products that can implement it - Source repo / configuration repo (Git-based) - GitHub (Repos) - GitLab (Repositories) - Bitbucket - Azure Repos
- Artifact repositories / registries (for images, packages, charts, bundles) - JFrog Artifactory - Sonatype Nexus Repository - Harbor (OCI registry)
- GitHub Packages / GitLab Package Registry - Azure Container Registry (ACR), AWS ECR, Google Artifact Registry
- Often paired “GitOps deployer” (not the repository, but commonly cooperates with it) - Argo CD - Flux CD

element.name: Fulfillment Book
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Fulfillment Book)) data object is a Runbook for the fulfillment of individual Resources as part of the Fulfillment Orchestration functional component. The plan can either be an automated template or/and manual process description.
*Key Attributes*
The Fulfillment Book data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier for the Fulfillment Book record * *Category:* helps in associating the Fulfillment Book to the Desired Product Instance * *Description:* description of the Fulfillment Book * *Execution Time:* date/time when the Fulfillment Book was created
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Fulfillment Book data object shall maintain the following relationship:
• Desired Product Instance to Fulfillment Book (m:n): every Desired Product Instance will have one or more fulfillment plans

element.name: Incident Management
element.documentation: The ability responsible for managing the Lifecycle of all Incidents. The primary Objective of Incident Management is to return the IT Service to Customers as quickly as possible.

element.name: Fulfillment
element.documentation:
<<<
*Purpose*
The ((Fulfillment)) functional component ensures that fulfillment activities are documented and automated for the resources that support the Digital Product Instance. Such activity items may include provisioning, deploying, modifying, actions (i.e., start, stop, etc.), decommissioning, and so on. This manages the routine deployment and configuration activities into automated/semi-automated Fulfillment Book. The automated/semi-automated fulfillment scripts are often included in the release package. The Fulfillment functional component may receive these deployment scripts from Release Composition functional component for the product deployment.
*Functional Criteria*
The Fulfillment functional component:
* Shall ensure the fulfillment activities are standardized, automated, or, if manually executed, well-documented for the resources that support the Digital Product Instance * Shall ensure that the automated Runbooks are updated with routine deployment and configuration activities including but not limited to building and deploying resources, configuring VMs, decommissioning VMs, configuring platforms, etc. * Shall monitor that the Fulfillment Books are being executed as per the designed specification * Can allow the Fulfillment Orchestration functional component to trigger automated Fulfillment Books for deploying or configuring resources underpinning a digital service in order to fulfill a service order

element.name: AI Platform Vendor Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to select, govern, and manage vendors contributing to the AI Platform, ensuring that external products and services align with platform strategy, architectural principles, compliance requirements, and long-term sustainability objectives.
This capability manages dependency risks, transparency expectations, and lifecycle alignment of vendor offerings.

element.name: Desired Policy State
element.documentation: A versioned, declarative representation of intended policies and their bindings (such as tenant, environment, or scope), serving as the system of record from which effective policy configurations are derived.

element.name: Model Serving Platform (Cloud)
element.documentation: “The Platform layer includes the software and tools required to develop, train, and deploy AI models.” p. 5 (Infrastructure and Platform Layers)

element.name: AI Platform Run Policy
element.documentation:
Govern how AI Platform capabilities may be executed at runtime, regardless of which AI solution is calling them.
Examples: - External vs internal model usage per risk class - PII/PHI handling rules, redaction requirements - Logging/audit obligations (retention, traceability) - Rate limits / quotas as policy constraints (e.g., “max rpm per tenant tier”), not the exact configured number - Safety norms and incident triggers (violation thresholds, escalation rules)
Note: exact values like top_k=7 or 200 rpm belong in Configuration, but the allowed range / tier rule can be a Policy.

element.name: Policy Version Reference
element.documentation: Minimal reference to “what policy version was active” (policy-id, bundle digest, git commit/tag, rollout id).

element.name: AI Platform Identity & Group Directory
element.documentation: Provides directory lookup and membership resolution services (e.g., user attributes, groups, roles) for identity federation and access control decisions.

element.name: Hergebruik voor kopen voor maken
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Ga uit van overheidsbreed hergebruik van diensten, of onderdelen daarvan voordat je overgaat tot het kopen of het laten maken.
Rationale Hergebruik van diensten of onderdelen daarvan is duurzaam en kostenefficiënt, en bovendien leidt dit tot standaardisering van dienstverlening en informatievoorzieningen.
Als hergebruik niet mogelijk is, ga dan na of aanpassing op het bestaande mogelijk is. Pas daarna komt het alternatief om iets te kopen of desnoods te (laten) maken in zicht.
Realiseert doel(-en) Duurzaam Innovatief Kostenefficiënt Uniform
Alle implicaties bij dit architectuurprincipe IMP021 Bevorder hergebruik van gegevens IMP027 Gebruik open source IMP074 Identificeer de voor de dienst relevante standaardoplossingen IMP085 Kies bij cloudoplossingen SaaS boven PaaS boven IaaS IMP028 Maak diensten herbruikbaar IMP024 Maak heldere afspraken over aanbieden en afnemen van diensten IMP003 Maak zoveel mogelijk data beschikbaar als open data IMP022 Neem oorspronkelijke grondslag mee bij hergebruik diensten IMP023 Regisseer hergebruik diensten in de keten IMP039 Zorg voor open specificaties

element.name: Plan & Approve Deployment
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Plan & Approve Deployment`"` is to create a deployment plan for a Digital Product and, based on this plan, to sign it off with all relevant stakeholders.
The details of the plan and the extent of approval required depends on the complexity of the deployment.
<<<

element.name: AI Platform Policy Administration
element.documentation:
Manages the complete lifecycle of policies as deployable artifacts: authoring/registration, versioning, validation, and publishing/promotion/rollback of policy bundles. Defines which policy set applies to which tenant, environment (dev/test/prod), platform capability, and/or solution. Provides control-plane APIs and/or UI for policy governance, traceability, and auditability (who/what/when/why).
aka: PAP

element.name: Service Level Management
element.documentation: The ability to define and negotiate service warranties and qualities of service that comprise standard or custom service level offerings, and to govern compliance to them. SLM is responsible for ensuring that all IT Service Management Processes, Operational Level Agreements, and Underpinning Contracts, are appropriate for the agreed Service Level Targets. SLM monitors and reports on Service Levels, and holds regular Customer reviews.

element.name: Model Artifact
element.documentation: “Version control for models and data … safe deployment strategies like canary releases to ensure reliability.” → Implies repository storing model versions and datasets. p. 8 (LLMOps section)

element.name: CI/CD Orchestrator
element.documentation: “Automated continuous integration/continuous delivery pipelines incorporating rigorous testing, version control for models and data.” p. 8

element.name: Policy Decision Log
element.documentation: Records PDP decisions per request (allow/deny + reason, policy-id, version, obligations/advice, tenant, subject, resource, action).

element.name: Finalize Roadmap & Scope Agreement
element.documentation: The purpose of the value stream stage "`Finalize Roadmap & Scope Agreement`" is to assign work and begin to conduct planning meetings on a regular cadence. Stakeholder buy-in and agreement to the Release roadmap and outcomes must be obtained. This includes setting up tracking and reporting on progress. The deliverables for this value stream stage are an approved Architecture Roadmap and outcomes.

element.name: AI Solution Policy Management
element.documentation:
Manage policies specific to one AI Solution (creation, review, approval, audit, exceptions) that define under which conditions AI capabilities may be used and which controls are mandatory.
Examples (for Archival solution): - When auto-classification is allowed vs when human review is mandatory (human-in-the-loop thresholds) - Which retention schedule/selectielijst is applicable per document category - Required evidence/explainability level for decisions - Segregation of duties (four-eyes for overrides) - Records management obligations for outputs and decision traces
Key constraint: AI Solution policies must not override platform policies; they must fit within them (platform = guardrails).

element.name: Log
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
A ((Log)) data object captures information related to CIs in order to better understand performance, health, and/or usage of an Actual Product Instance.
*Key Attributes*
The Log data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier of a Log record * *Timestamp:* time of the Log event * *Data:* collected Log text
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Log data object shall maintain the following relationships:
* Log to Event (n:m): according to monitoring policies, some Log events are forwarded as service events * Log to Service Monitor (n:1): associated Log to the Service Monitor definition that enables the collection of the Log record

element.name: AI Policy Definition & Conformance Process
element.documentation: Define internal AI policies and technical standards. Communicate them to stakeholders, and monitor adherence through reviews and conformance assessments. Update policies based on regulatory or technological changes.

element.name: AI Stakeholder & Consumer Relationship Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to establish, maintain, and evolve relationships with internal and external consumers and stakeholders of AI platform services, ensuring alignment between business objectives, AI platform capabilities, governance requirements, and expected value.
This capability manages expectations, clarifies responsibilities between the AI platform and AI solutions, and provides structured input into strategy, portfolio, and value management based on stakeholder needs and feedback.
Key AI-specific aspects - Engagement with diverse stakeholder groups (business owners, AI solution teams, governance bodies, external consumers) - Translation of business needs into AI platform service requirements - Alignment of expectations regarding responsibility, risk ownership, and compliance

element.name: Policy Bundle
element.documentation: A versioned, runtime-ready package of policy definitions and related metadata, produced from the desired policy state and consumed by policy decision components for enforcement.

element.name: AI Integration & Interface Enablement Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide standardized interfaces, API frameworks, security models, and integration patterns that allow AI Solutions to be exposed and consumed by applications in a controlled and consistent manner.
This capability ensures interoperability and secure integration without dictating application design.
Purpose - Ensure standardized exposure of AI Solutions - Reduce integration complexity - Protect platform boundaries - Maintain security and governance consistency

element.name: Knowledge Management
element.documentation: The ability to gather, analyze, store and share knowledge and information within an Organization. The primary purpose of Knowledge Management is to improve Efficiency by reducing the need to rediscover knowledge.

element.name: Policy Telemetry Collector
element.documentation: Ingests decision logs and enforcement events (push/pull), normalizes, enriches (tenant, policy version, service).

element.name: Datakwaliteit en Consistentie
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Data en kennis moeten accuraat, volledig en consistent zijn gedurende de gehele levenscyclus.
Rationale AI-output is slechts zo goed als de kwaliteit van de input; inconsistentie leidt tot incorrecte resultaten.
Consequenties Klant levert gevalideerde data en goedgekeurde modellen. Automatische kwaliteitscontroles en rapportages. Data met onvoldoende status in quarantaine/staging. DQI’s worden formeel vastgelegd.

element.name: Validate Deployment
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Validate Deployment`" is to ensure that all aspects of the deployment activities were correctly executed. This includes checking that the Product Instance is indeed running and configured according to plan, and that the monitoring and documentation associated is configured and made available.

element.name: Identity and Access Management
element.documentation: The ability to design, develop manage, and control the security of automation services and information to meet requirements. The capability to ensure the Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability of an Organization’s Assets, information, data and IT Services. Authorization and Access Management usually forms part of an organizational approach to Security Management that has a wider scope than the IT Service Provider, and includes handling of paper, building access, phone calls, etc., for the entire organization.

element.name: Policy Effectiveness Metrics
element.documentation: KPIs such as deny-rate by policy/tenant, latency impact, false-positive indicators, override rate, top rules triggered.

element.name: AI Platform Capacity Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to plan, monitor, and manage the capacity of AI Platform resources to meet current and future demand, covering not only infrastructure resources but also data processing capacity and governance throughput.
Capacity management ensures that platform growth remains sustainable and that constrained resources (such as review capacity or high-risk usage allowances) do not become unmanaged bottlenecks.

element.name: Capacity Management
element.documentation: The ability to ensure that the Capacity of IT Services and the IT Infrastructure is able to deliver agreed Service Level Targets in a Cost Effective and timely manner. Capacity Management considers all Resources required to deliver the IT Service, and plans for short-, medium- and long-term Business Requirements. The Resources used by each IT Service and the pattern of usage over time are collected, recorded, and analyzed for use in the Capacity Plan.

element.name: Identity Provider
element.documentation: “Stringent access controls … continuous monitoring for anomalous activity … specialized security testing.” p. 9

element.name: AI Platform Requirements Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to capture, refine, manage, and trace requirements for the AI Platform across their lifecycle, ensuring alignment with strategy, architecture, policy, and compliance constraints.
This capability maintains traceability from strategic intent and regulatory requirements to platform design, implementation, testing, and release artifacts.
What is AI-platform specific: - Requirements are often multi-layered: - functional (services, APIs) - non-functional (performance, availability) - governance (auditability, oversight) - ethical or regulatory interpretations - Traceability is essential for accountability and assurance - Requirements may evolve due to: - regulatory clarification - emerging risks - platform learning

element.name: Domain Expert
element.documentation: “Human-in-the-loop (HITL) approach … positioning humans as valuable guides when agents encounter limitations.” (p7)

element.name: Embedding generation
element.documentation: Generates vector representations of text, images, or other data at runtime, typically used for semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and similarity matching.

element.name: Operate AI Platform Process
element.documentation: Operate and monitor the AI platform’s infrastructure components (compute, networking, storage, orchestration). Manage incidents, configuration, and capacity to ensure availability and resilience.

element.name: Volledige Traceerbaarheid
element.documentation: Elke AI-interactie is herleidbaar naar data, modelversie, policyversie en tenantcontext.

element.name: Model Serving Service (Hosted LLM)
element.documentation:
same passage — offers the deployment capability. p. 5
Generic LLM Execution Service

element.name: Informeer bij de bron
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Maak bij de dienst gebruik van gegevens die afkomstig zijn uit een bronregistratie.
Rationale Voor betrouwbare dienstverlening is het hergebruik van de juiste informatie en documenten van cruciaal belang. Om de kwaliteit over de juistheid van een gegeven te borgen, moet duidelijk zijn welke organisatie bepaalt wat de juiste informatie is. Uitgangspunt is dat er voor gegevens waar de overheid gebruik van maakt, altijd één bron bestaat, die leidend is.
Realiseert doel(-en) Noodzakelijk Uniform Wendbaar
Alle implicaties bij dit architectuurprincipe IMP060 Bepaal autorisaties met metadata IMP021 Bevorder hergebruik van gegevens IMP059 Geef de voorkeur aan halen i.p.v. brengen van gegevens IMP055 Gegevens eenmalig uitgevraagd, uniek opgeslagen, meervoudig gebruikt IMP033 Koppel bronsystemen op basis van een passende classificatie IMP056 Registreer gegevens bij de bron IMP077 Stel de juridische aansprakelijkheid per gegevensobject vast IMP058 Stel voor ieder gegeven de unieke bron vast IMP007 Verwijs naar de bron IMP057 Weet welke (bron)gegevens in huis zijn

element.name: AI Solution Strategy to Portfolio Support
Evaluation Service
element.documentation: “Comprehensive testing strategy … essential for building dependable GenAI solutions.” p. 9–10

element.name: Digitale Soevereiniteit
element.documentation:
Beschrijving De organisatie behoudt controle over data, modellen, infrastructuur en kritieke AI-capabilities.
Rationale Ongecontroleerde afhankelijkheid van externe leveranciers kan leiden tot juridische, operationele en geopolitieke risico’s.
Eerste implicaties - Bewuste cloud/on-prem keuzes - Exit-strategieën - Transparantie over modelherkomst - Gebruik van open standaarden

element.name: Response Delivery
element.documentation: The answer is presented to the user;

element.name: LLMOps Service
element.documentation: “LLMOps provides the essential framework for automating and managing the GenAI model lifecycle.” p. 8

element.name: Model Lifecycle Management Service
element.documentation: “Design, develop, test, evaluate, deploy, and continuously monitor and improve generative AI solutions.” p. 4, 8

element.name: AI Platform Operations Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to operate and control the day-to-day execution of AI Platform services and pipelines in accordance with defined policies, architecture, and service levels.
Operations management ensures stable, predictable platform behavior while respecting governance constraints and enabling rapid response to detected issues.

element.name: Betrouwbare en Beheersbare AI executie
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Het platform borgt gecontroleerde, voorspelbare en begrensde uitvoering van AI-workloads binnen vooraf gedefinieerde operationele en beleidsmatige kaders.
Rationale AI-workloads zijn dynamisch en kunnen onverwacht gedrag vertonen; beheersbaarheid voorkomt ongewenste effecten.
Implicaties - Contextbegrenzing: AI krijgt alleen toegang tot expliciet toegestane context (bijv. beperkte datasets, tokenlimieten). - Human override hooks: Kritische beslissingen kunnen door mensen worden overruled of gevalideerd. - Fallback-mechanismen: Bij fouten of onzekerheid wordt teruggevallen op alternatieve logica (bijv. rule-based of menselijke afhandeling). - Outputvalidatie: Resultaten van AI worden gecontroleerd op kwaliteit, veiligheid en consistentie voordat ze gebruikt worden. - Guardrails en safety controls: Filters en regels voorkomen ongewenste output (bijv. bias, schadelijke content). - Kosten- en gebruikscontrole: Limieten op gebruik (rate limits, budgetten) voorkomen ongecontroleerde kosten. - Monitoring van afwijkend gedrag: Detectie van afwijkingen in output of performance (bijv. drift of hallucinations).

element.name: Betrouwbaarheid en Accuratesse
element.documentation:
Beschrijving AI-systemen leveren aantoonbaar betrouwbare en consistente uitkomsten binnen hun beoogde toepassingsgebied.
Rationale AI-output kan feitelijk onjuist of inconsistent zijn, ook bij correcte data. Betrouwbaarheid is essentieel voor vertrouwen en kwaliteit.
Eerste implicaties - Validatie voorafgaand aan inzet - Monitoring van outputkwaliteit - Duidelijke afbakening van toepassingsdomein - Communicatie over onzekerheid

element.name: On-Premise Data Center
element.documentation: “On-Premises (Self-Managed LLM): Dedicated hardware … provides full control.” p. 5

element.name: Customer/User Support
element.documentation: The ability to assist users with interactions to service support. The support capability is be provided via varity of media (e.g. click, call, face), assists VIP's, allows for self-help, has the ability to list current disruptions and routes interactions to the correct supporting agents.

element.name: Is uitlegbaar, transparant en herleidbaar
element.documentation:
RVO is transparant over de inzet van AI en algoritmen.
Implicaties - RVO draagt de verantwoordelijkheid informatie over de inzet van AI en algoritmen voor een breed publiek toegankelijk en begrijpelijk te maken. - AI-systemen en algoritmen moeten uitlegbaar en herleidbaar zijn om controleerbaarheid te waarborgen en zo een passende rechtsgang mogelijk te maken in geval van fouten.
Bron: Governancekader Verantwoorde inzet algoritmen en AI

element.name: Gather Influencers
element.documentation:
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Gather Influencers`" is to bring together inputs for a planning cycle. Typical inputs include: consumer demand, improvement ideas, new technology opportunities, technology lifecycle events (e.g., end-of-life), and any known policy (legal and regulatory demands) or budget constraints for the Digital Product Portfolio. Other inputs may include an internal and/or external environmental scan to identify strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. Discussion with the business is centered on Digital Product value delivery for consumers and corresponding policy requirements. The deliverable for this value stream stage is new or updated Strategic Themes and/or Strategic Objectives.
An important part of this stage is to manage the strategic roadmap of the Digital Enterprise. Throughout the IT4IT Value Streams, this roadmap will be refined from the Strategic intent to the Architecture Roadmap to the Digital Product Roadmap to the Release Roadmap.
<<<
<<<

element.name: Policy Violation / Incident Record
element.documentation: A detected or reported violation (bypass, misconfiguration, drift, unexpected allow, policy gap), with severity and owner.

element.name: On-premise GPU Compute Cluster
element.documentation: “Dedicated hardware (e.g., Nvidia DGX, graphics processing unit [GPU] clusters) provides full control but requires high maintenance.” p. 5 (“Infrastructure and Platform Layers”)

element.name: Bescherming van Persoonsgegevens
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Persoonsgegevens worden verwerkt conform geldende wetgeving (AVG) met privacy by design en by default als uitgangspunt.
Rationale Privacy is een grondrecht. AI-systemen vergroten het risico op hergebruik, inferentie en ongecontroleerde verspreiding van persoonsgegevens.
Eerste implicaties - DPIA bij risicovolle toepassingen - Dataminimalisatie - Doelbinding expliciet vastgelegd - Beperkte retentie en gecontroleerde doorgifte

element.name: Configuration Management
element.documentation: The ability to maintain information about Configuration Items required to deliver an IT Service, including their Relationships. This information is managed throughout the Lifecycle of the CI.

element.name: Build request context
element.documentation:
Request context is built incrementally. A preliminary context (tenant/route/action/correlation) is constructed before token validation to support correct routing and enforcement. After token/session validation, the context is enriched with verified identity claims and attributes for authorization and policy evaluation.
This step should: - Extract technical context - Extract declared context - Not yet trust identity claims
Examples of what can happen before validation: - Extract tenant ID from URL / header - Extract requested action type - Extract solution ID - Extract purpose (if declared) - Correlate request ID

element.name: Availability Management
element.documentation: The ability to define, analyze, Plan, measure and improve all aspects of the Availability of IT Services. Availability Management is responsible for ensuring that all IT Infrastructure, Processes, Tools, Roles, etc. are appropriate for the agreed Service Level Targets for Availability.

element.name: AI Policy Administration Service
element.documentation: Stores versioned policy bundles, tenant/environment scoping, lifecycle state (draft/published/deprecated), policy metadata.

element.name: Delivering intelligent, reliable, and ethically sound applications that drive measurable business outcomes
Data Engineer
element.documentation: “Enterprise GenAI applications rely on high-quality, well-structured data … data pipelines must address preprocessing, transformation, integration.” (p6)

element.name: Model output bias detection
element.documentation: Monitors live model outputs to detect unfair or skewed behavior across protected or sensitive attributes.

element.name: AI Platform Policy Compliance & Validation
element.documentation:
Description Provides automated, machine-executable validation services that enable AI Solutions to verify conformance with AI Platform policies during design, build, and deployment. The function evaluates solution artifacts (such as solution descriptors, declared capabilities, and orchestration plans) against the applicable policy sets defined and governed by Policy Administration. It produces deterministic validation outcomes and compliance evidence that can be used as CI/CD gates and deployment admission criteria.
Responsibilities - Determines the applicable policy set for a given solution, tenant, environment, and platform capability. - Validates AI Solution artifacts against platform policies, standards, and structural constraints. - Ensures validation is repeatable and traceable by supporting policy version pinning. - Produces machine-readable validation reports indicating compliance, violations, and required remediation. - Optionally issues signed compliance attestations that can be used as evidence for deployment gating and audit.
Key characteristics - Designed for automated consumption by CI/CD pipelines and deployment admission controllers. - Consumes policy bundles and applicability mappings governed by Policy Administration; does not author or modify policies itself. - Complements runtime policy decision and enforcement by reducing the policy violation surface before deployment. - Enables solution self-validation in alignment with platform governance and DevOps/GitOps practices.
Typical inputs and outputs
Inputs: Solution artifacts (descriptors, orchestration plans), target environment/tenant context, policy bundle reference.
Outputs: Validation report, compliance status, optional attestation or evidence record.
Relation to other policy functions - Reads policy bundles and applicability rules published by AI Platform Policy Administration. - Supports deployment and admission processes that precede Policy Delivery & Synchronization. - Operates independently of Policy Decision and runtime enforcement functions, which apply policies using execution-time context.

element.name: Build Package
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Build Package)) data object represents a collection of build artifacts (components) packaged into a deployable unit. The Build Packages are securely stored in a build/artifact repository.
*Key Attributes*
The Build Package data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier of the Build Package * *Name:* meaningful name of the Build Package * *Description:* short description of the Build Package * *Type:* define the Build Package type; e.g., container image * *Status:* lifecycle status of the Build Package * *Compliance Status:* compliance status of the Build Packages (and their components) * *Version:* version of the Build Package * *Tags and Metadata:* a build can have one or more tags (or properties) to provide additional metadata
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Build Package data object shall maintain the following relationships:
* Source to Build Package (n:m): Source can be built multiple times to create several build versions * Build Package to Test Plan (n:m): one or many Build Packages can be related to one or many Test Plans used as part of the build creation (e.g., perform security and vulnerability scanning on the new or modified Build Packages) * Build Package to Product Release Blueprint (n:m): multiple Build Packages, which represent the deployable units or components, can be assembled into a Product Release Blueprint which is used for the deployment of the Product Release into the different environments * Build Package to Pipeline (n:1): a Build Package is associated with the Pipeline through which the Build Package is created (or modified) * Build Package to Build Package (n:m): a Build Package can have dependencies with other Build Packages (or components)

element.name: Policy
element.documentation:
<<< *Purpose* The ((Policy)) functional component manages the creation, review, approval, and audit of Policies.
*Functional Criteria* The Policy functional component: * Shall align and map to Enterprise Architecture * Shall enable the review and approval of Policies based on roles and responsibilities * Shall provide visibility into Policy attributes such as types, status, non-compliance, audit history, risks, and issues * Shall manage the governance of Policies associated with downstream value stream stages of the product lifecycle * Shall manage both internal and external Policies (e.g., security, pricing/costing, resources, regulatory, risk, information) and history of revisions * Shall track Policy exceptions and waivers with consistent identification, evaluation, and status reports for managing corrective actions
*Data Architecture Criteria* The Policy functional component: * Shall associate one or more Policies to one or more Portfolio Backlog Items in the Portfolio * Shall associate one or more Policies to one or more work packages on the Architecture Roadmap

element.name: Fulfillment Orchestration
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Fulfillment Orchestration)) functional component orchestrates the delivery of various Orders across one or more Fulfillment functional components in order to fulfill the service orders (such as the provision of VMs, modification of resource allocation, addition/removal of capacity, patches installation, modification of access rights, etc.) that are triggered by:
* Release Composition to deploy new Product Releases received from the Integrate value stream * Order (e.g., end-user request from the portal, application access request, password reset, etc. ) * Change (e.g., provision of the test environment, deployment of bug-fix, deployment of the patch, etc. ) * Pipeline (for continuous deployment)
The Fulfillment Orchestration may orchestrate through multiple internal and/or external Fulfillment functional components to automatically execute a fulfillment process workflow. To be able to engage the fulfillers, the Fulfillment Orchestration functional component needs to:
* Manage a registry of the available fulfillers; this registry captures: ** What each fulfiller does (capabilities) ** How to engage each fulfiller (where they are located and how to invoke them) * Take the released Offer template deployment recipe from Release Composition and update the change request (for fulfillment) and also the Desired Product Instance data object that represents the service model in its preconfigured state * Get the resources allocated that are required to fulfill the service order * Update the resource pool inventory as resources are provisioned * Based on the new or updated Desired Product Instance and the result of fulfilling the Change (Change data object), inform the Configuration functional component (if needed)
The Fulfillment Orchestration functional component can be used via two paradigms:
* Consumer-driven + In this paradigm, a consumer request results in a bound catalog item that is broken down into the necessary Change Orders needed to fulfill the originating request; typically, this is for delivering the consumption of service from an existing product/service system that has previously been deployed. * Pipeline-driven + In this paradigm, the Fulfillment Orchestration functional component is directly engaged by the Pipeline functional component; this paradigm is used when the Development team is ready to transition a product into the production environment and wants to deploy a service system for the product.
*Functional Criteria*
The Fulfillment Orchestration functional component:
* Shall orchestrate the implementation of the various Change Orders across the fulfillment systems, or track and manage manual change task implementation to fulfill the Orders * Update the resource inventory as resources are ordered * May manage a registry of the available fulfillers to include what each fulfiller does (capabilities) and how to engage each fulfiller (where they are located and how to invoke them) * May take the bound Offer template from the Change functional component and generate both the relevant Fulfillment Orders to realize/fulfill the originating consumer request and the Desired Product Instance data object, which represents the system in its preconfigured state * Shall get the technology resources (such as license, IP, VMs, etc.) allocated to fulfill the service order from the Resource functional component * Shall select the appropriate fulfillment mechanism * Shall coordinate if multiple fulfillment mechanisms are needed, and manage the dependencies required to fulfill the digital service orders * Shall create a Desired Product Instance based on a template in the Product Release package if the Order is a request for a new product system * Should modify the associated Desired Product Instance for all consumer Subscriptions to the service in order to track service delivery * Shall provide the Subscription status to the Order functional component * Shall create the Actual Product Instance as a copy of the Desired Product Instance within the Configuration functional component * May update the resource pool inventory as resources are provisioned *
