element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to support the structured onboarding and enablement of consumers (applications, teams, or user groups) for approved AI Solutions, ensuring that activation is consistent, traceable, and aligned with governance conditions.
This capability ensures that AI Solutions are not directly exposed without controlled activation mechanisms.
Purpose - Ensure controlled activation of AI Solutions - Prevent unauthorized or premature exposure - Establish clear accountability for consumer usage - Provide structured onboarding pathways

element.name: Performance Management
element.documentation: The ability responsible for day-to-day Performance Management Activities. These include monitoring, threshold detection, Performance analysis and Tuning, and implementing changes related to Performance and Capacity.

element.name: AI Platform Policy Delivery & Synchronization
element.documentation:
Ensures that the correct policy artifacts produced by Policy Administration are made available to the appropriate runtimes and enforcement points, at the right time and in a controlled manner. Supports push and pull distribution models, caching, version synchronization, staged rollout/canary deployments, rollback, drift detection, and integrity controls (e.g. signature or attestation verification). Makes policy propagation observable and auditable by tracking which policy version is active where, since when, and whether it matches the intended state.
Design choice: Policy Delivery & Synchronization may be realized either as a declarative GitOps reconciliation mechanism or as an event-driven control-plane push mechanism. The choice determines where desired state is stored (Git vs control plane) and how convergence is achieved (reconciliation vs notification).

element.name: Software Engineer
element.documentation: “Platform layer includes the software and tools required to develop, train, and deploy AI models.” (p5)

element.name: AI Solution Detect to Correct Support
AI Platform Collaboration Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to enable structured and effective collaboration between stakeholders involved in the AI Platform, including platform teams, governance bodies, and professional consumers, ensuring shared understanding, traceable decisions, and coordinated platform evolution.
This capability supports cross-disciplinary collaboration where technical, legal, ethical, and business perspectives must be aligned.

element.name: Product Development
element.documentation: The ability to implement new or changed software services application and data components by coding, code unit verification and defect correction.

element.name: Data Quality & Preparation Service
element.documentation: “High-quality, robust, diverse, and complete data … preprocessing, transformation, integration.” p. 6

element.name: AI Policy Distribution Service
element.documentation:
Serves policy bundles to runtimes (pull) and/or triggers updates (push), supports caching and version pinning.
Alternative: Runtime Policy Agent/Sidecar (Runs near enforcement points to fetch/verify/cache bundles and provide a local decision interface.)
Design decision: Argo CD only for pure infrastuctural changes and separate product (e.g. OPAL) for policy changes, or everything with Argo CD.

element.name: Zet rechtvaardigheid centraal
element.documentation:
Reden - Het zijn van een betrouwbare partner voor onze klanten en opdrachtgevers
Implicaties - AI en algoritmen moet worden ontworpen en ingezet op een manier die rechtvaardigheid waarborgt en rechtmatig is. - Discriminatie is te allen tijde verboden op grond van artikel 14 EVRM en artikel 1 Grondwet. - Dit betekent ook het vermijden van vooroordelen en het bevorderen van inclusieve praktijken die rekening houden met de behoeften en rechten van alle individuen en gemeenschappen, ongeacht hun kenmerken.
Bron: Governancekader Verantwoorde inzet algoritmen en AI

element.name: AI Platform Observability Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to generate, collect, correlate, and present logs, metrics, traces, events, and alerts across platform services and supported AI Solutions in order to provide operational visibility and diagnostic insight.
This capability ensures that both platform teams and solution teams can understand behavior, performance, failures, and anomalies through consistent observability mechanisms rather than fragmented monitoring point solutions.
Purpose - Provide end-to-end visibility into platform and solution behavior - Support timely detection, diagnosis, and analysis of issues - Enable evidence-based operational improvement through logs, metrics, traces, and alerts - Strengthen reliability and control through consistent observability practices

element.name: Respect voor Menselijke Autonomie
element.documentation:
Beschrijving AI-systemen worden ontworpen en ingezet op een wijze die menselijke besluitvorming ondersteunt en het zelfbeschikkingsrecht van individuen respecteert.
Rationale AI mag menselijke oordeelsvorming niet vervangen waar fundamentele belangen in het geding zijn. Dit principe beschermt autonomie en voorkomt manipulatief of disproportioneel gebruik van AI.
Eerste implicaties - Betekenisvolle menselijke tussenkomst waar passend - Heldere afbakening van menselijke en geautomatiseerde besluitvorming - Mogelijkheid tot menselijke interventie of correctie

element.name: Subscribe to Service Offer
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Subscribe to Service Offer`" is to make the Actual Product Instance, as result of the Order and Desired Product Instance, available to the customer, in such a way that the customer is able to consume the Digital Product.

element.name: AI Platform Customer / User Support Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to support professional consumers of the AI Platform by resolving usage issues, explaining platform behavior and constraints, and assisting with correct and responsible use of platform capabilities and knowledge assets.
This capability provides expert enablement support rather than end-user application support and escalates issues to governance or oversight functions where appropriate.
What is AI-platform specific: - Support questions often relate to: - interpretation of platform behavior - limitations of AI services - governance or compliance constraints - Emphasis on: - transparency - responsible usage guidance - escalation to oversight when needed

element.name: Compliance & Audit Platform
element.documentation: “Bias Detection and Mitigation … using specialized tools to audit datasets for representation gaps.” p. 9 (AI GRC)

element.name: Measurement & Reporting
element.documentation: Measurement & Reporting is the ability to consolidate information across the various value streams and functional components, to assess and evaluate metrics and KPIs, and provide service reporting, analytics, dashboards and scorecards.

element.name: AI Threat & Vulnerability Management Process
element.documentation:
Identify, analyze, and mitigate AI-specific threats such as data poisoning, adversarial attacks, and model exfiltration. Coordinate with enterprise SOC and risk management teams.
GRC section on security “Prompt Injection … Data Poisoning … Mitigation requires robust input validation and sanitization, output filtering …” p. 9

element.name: GPU Compute Service (Cloud)
element.documentation: “This computational infrastructure is at the foundation of any GenAI system. Enterprises must evaluate three primary deployment models … scalable compute resources.” p. 5

element.name: AI Deployment Readiness & Packaging Support Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to support the preparation of AI Solutions for controlled release and activation by ensuring configuration completeness, traceability, versioning, and alignment with platform standards before transition to Request-to-Fulfill.
This capability ensures that AI Solutions can be safely and coherently enabled for consumers once approved.
Purpose - Prevent incomplete or non-compliant deployment - Ensure versioned and traceable solution configurations - Align solution readiness with platform release standards - Support smooth transition into operational enablement

element.name: AI Solution Governance Compliance Monitoring Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to continuously assess whether AI Solutions remain compliant with approved governance conditions, documentation requirements, oversight intensity, and usage constraints during operation.
This capability ensures that runtime behavior remains aligned with prior approval and regulatory expectations.
Purpose - Prevent divergence from approved scope - Maintain regulatory and ethical posture - Provide compliance evidence - Support intervention when necessary

element.name: AI Platform Problem Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to identify, analyze, and resolve the underlying causes of recurring or significant issues affecting the AI Platform, including technical faults, data quality issues, governance failures, and unintended system behavior.
Problem management focuses on structural remediation rather than immediate restoration, feeding corrective actions into platform backlog, architecture, and policy adjustments.

element.name: AI Solution Drift Detection & Stability Monitoring Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to detect and signal degradation in performance, reliability, or behavioral stability of AI Solutions due to data changes, usage shifts, configuration drift, or evolving operational conditions.
This capability identifies structural instability without redefining solution goals.
Purpose - Detect data or behavioral drift - Surface declining effectiveness patterns - Prevent unnoticed quality erosion - Inform corrective redesign decisions

element.name: Accept & Publish Release
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Accept and Publish Release`" is to accept the Product Release as a candidate for deployment into production. The required change authority, such as the Product Manager, accepts the Product Release for deployment and/or release into production. The Product Manager accepts the Product Release after engaging with relevant stakeholders, which includes reviewing test results to accept or reject the list of remaining Defects, and reviewing the collateral produced. In a DevOps model, Operations has been participating all along but at this point an approval is given for the Product Release to be deployed into production.
This value stream stage ensures only validated and approved release packages can be deployed into production. The release package is tagged and secured to prevent any modification at a later stage (e.g., to prevent unauthorized changes being made).

element.name: AI Strategy & Governance Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to define, communicate, and continuously steer the strategic vision, objectives, and governance model of the AI Platform, ensuring that its evolution, positioning, and investment decisions are aligned with enterprise strategy, societal responsibilities, and regulatory context.
This capability establishes the strategic intent, guiding principles, and accountability structures for the AI Platform, and provides directional guidance for policy, architecture, portfolio, and investment decisions—without managing operational execution or solution-level outcomes.
The purpose of the AI Platform Strategy & Governance Capability is to: - Articulate the long-term vision and role of the AI Knowledge Platform within the enterprise - Define strategic objectives and success criteria for platform adoption and value - Establish governance structures, ownership, and decision rights at platform level - Provide a coherent strategic frame for policy, architecture, and portfolio management

element.name: LLMOps & Versioning Component
element.documentation: “LLMOps provides the essential framework for automating and managing the GenAI model lifecycle … feedback loops and retraining … CI/CD pipelines … canary releases.” p. 8 (LLMOps section)

element.name: Model Version Release Process
element.documentation: Manage the controlled release of AI models across environments (development, staging, production). Ensure reproducibility, rollback capability, and compliance with release policies.

element.name: Explainability Reporting Process
element.documentation: Generate model documentation and transparency reports (e.g., model cards, decision traces, interpretability summaries). Provide stakeholders with understandable insights into model reasoning and limitations.

element.name: AI Collaborative Workspace Enablement Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide shared workspaces, project contexts, and collaborative working environments in which multiple users can jointly work on AI-related artefacts, configurations, knowledge assets, and outputs in a coordinated manner.
This capability ensures that the platform supports teamwork around AI Solutions and AI-enabled work, rather than only individual interaction with isolated tools or artefacts.
Purpose - Enable collaborative work across teams and roles within shared project contexts - Support coordinated use of documents, configurations, and AI outputs - Reduce fragmentation of work across disconnected tools and personal environments - Improve continuity, shared understanding, and productivity in AI-enabled work

element.name: AI Solution Design Enablement Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide architectural patterns, configurable templates, reusable components, and design guidance that support the structured realization of AI Solutions within the platform.
This capability ensures that AI Solutions are designed in alignment with platform architecture, governance mechanisms, and integration standards.
Purpose - Accelerate structured AI Solution design - Reduce architectural fragmentation - Ensure compatibility with platform services - Embed governance-by-design principles

element.name: Informatiebeveiliging en Cyberweerbaarheid
element.documentation:
Beschrijving AI-systemen worden structureel beschermd tegen ongeautoriseerde toegang, manipulatie en verstoring.
Rationale AI vergroot de attack surface (prompt injection, data-exfiltration, model poisoning). Structurele beveiliging is noodzakelijk.
Eerste implicaties - BIO-conforme maatregelen - Secure SDLC - RBAC en scheiding van taken - Continue monitoring

element.name: AI Platform Workforce Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to plan, develop, and sustain the workforce required to design, govern, operate, and evolve the AI Platform, ensuring the availability of appropriate skills, roles, and responsibilities across technical, data, and governance domains.
This capability recognizes AI-specific roles and competencies and supports clear separation of duties between platform development, operation, and oversight.

element.name: GPU Compute Service (On-Premise)
element.documentation: “This computational infrastructure is at the foundation of any GenAI system. Enterprises must evaluate three primary deployment models … scalable compute resources.” p. 5

element.name: Model Serving Platform (On-premise)
element.documentation: “The Platform layer includes the software and tools required to develop, train, and deploy AI models.” p. 5 (Infrastructure and Platform Layers)

element.name: AI Platform Policy Decision
element.documentation:
Evaluates policies and produces policy decisions and obligations (e.g. allow/deny with conditions) for enforcement points. Supports request-time decisions (per API call, model invocation, or tool call) as well as pre-computed or batch decisions (e.g. entitlement assignments). Consumes contextual information such as tenant, user, solution, environment, resource attributes, classifications, and runtime signals. Exposes a consistent decision API and supports logging and explainability of decisions.
aka: PDP

element.name: Policy Feedback Orchestrator
element.documentation: Turns findings into actions: opens feedback requests, triggers rollback signals, notifies policy admins, raises incidents.

element.name: Scenario- Solving an Incident using Known Error information
Scheiding Data- en Modelinterfaces
element.documentation: Data retrieval en modelaanroepen zijn strikt gescheiden.

element.name: Attribute Records
element.documentation:
Typical attribute domains:
- Tenant attributes (tenant tier, enabled features) - Solution attributes (approved use cases, purpose binding) - Model attributes (risk class, deployment zone, cost class) - Data attributes (classification, PII flag, sensitivity) - Regulatory flags (high-risk AI, human-in-the-loop required)

element.name: Security Monitoring & Incident Response
element.documentation: Collects and analyzes security telemetry, integrates with SIEM/SOAR tools, issues alerts, and supports forensics and threat intelligence.

element.name: Certificate & Trust Management
element.documentation: Provides certificate lifecycle, PKI, TLS, and code-signing; enables mTLS between components.

element.name: AI Solution Strategic & Governance Advisory Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide structured strategic alignment and value–risk advisory support for proposed AI Solutions, integrating enterprise objectives, platform positioning, regulatory obligations, governance intensity, and operational impact into coherent decision guidance.
This capability applies platform principles and guardrails in context, enabling balanced portfolio decisions without assuming ownership of prioritization or outcomes.
Purpose - Ensure alignment of AI Solutions with enterprise and platform strategy - Make governance obligations and regulatory exposure visible early - Support balanced value-versus-risk trade-offs - Provide consistent decision criteria across AI proposals - Prevent underestimation of platform and compliance impact

element.name: Testing & Validation Service
element.documentation: “Testing, Validation, and Reliability Assurance … comprehensive testing strategy.” p. 9–10

element.name: Key & Secrets Management
element.documentation: Manages credentials, API keys, tokens, and encryption keys; enforces key rotation and access policies.

element.name: AI Solution Request to Fulfill Support
Test Plan
element.documentation:
<<<
*Purpose*
The ((Test Plan)) data object is a dynamic plan defining the test scope, test coverage, and associated Test Cases to be executed as part of new builds and/or releases. Multiple Test Plans can be created for a Digital Product. The Test Plan ensures that all required Test Cases are executed and verified as part of a Product Release.
*Key Attributes*
The Test Plan data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier of the Test Plan * *Name:* meaningful name for the Test Plan * *Description:* summary or short description of the Test Plan * *Status:* lifecycle status of the Test Plan * *Version:* version of the Test Plan * *Test Runs:* overview of Test Plan executions
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Test Case data object shall maintain the following relationships:
* Test Case to Test Plan (n:m): one or more Test Cases are related to one or more Test Plans * Product Release to Test Plan (n:m): a Product Release can have one or more associated Test Plans * Test Plan to Pipeline (n:1): a Test Plan can be associated to and initiated by one Pipeline * Test Plan to Test Plan (n:m): a Test Plan can link to another Test Plan (decomposition or relationship) * Test Plan to Build Package (n:m): a Test Plan can be created to scan and verify one or more Build Packages (e.g., for software compliance and potential vulnerabilities)

element.name: Compute Service
element.documentation: Implied by “Cloud platforms … deploy models with scalable compute resources.” Represents elastic compute capacity offered to applications. p. 5

element.name: Expliciete afweging van hergebruik, productinzet en bouw
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Voor iedere nieuwe capability of wijziging in het AI-kennisplatform wordt de oplossingskeuze expliciet en aantoonbaar onderbouwd. Daarbij wordt systematisch vastgesteld of hergebruik van bestaande bouwblokken mogelijk is, of een standaardproduct voldoende geschikt is, of dat bouwen noodzakelijk is.
Rationale Zonder expliciete afweging ontstaat het risico dat teams te snel kiezen voor bouwen, of hergebruik en productopties slechts beperkt onderzoeken. Een transparante en toetsbare afweging vergroot kostenefficiëntie, standaardisatie, beheersbaarheid en herbruikbaarheid, en voorkomt onnodige complexiteit in het platform.
Implicaties - Verplichte oplossingsafweging: Voor elke nieuwe capability wordt expliciet beoordeeld: hergebruik → product → bouw. - Inventarisatie van bestaande bouwblokken: Beschikbare interne en externe oplossingen worden systematisch onderzocht. - Marktverkenning: Er wordt aantoonbaar gekeken naar bestaande producten voordat bouw wordt overwogen. - Zelfbouw alleen met motivatie: Bouwen is alleen toegestaan met expliciete onderbouwing waarom andere opties niet voldoen. - Open source assemblage = bouwen: Samenstellen van oplossingen uit losse open source componenten wordt als bouw beschouwd. - Gestandaardiseerde beoordelingscriteria: Beslissingen worden genomen op basis van o.a. functionaliteit, kosten (TCO), beheerbaarheid, security en interoperabiliteit. - Transparante besluitvorming: Keuzes worden vastgelegd en zijn toetsbaar voor governance. - Architectuurreview verplicht bij bouwkeuze: Bouwbesluiten worden expliciet beoordeeld door architectuur- of governance-organen.

element.name: Deployment Management
element.documentation: Making a released product increment available to a specific consumer, environment, or context.

element.name: Content Safety Service
element.documentation: Same section — provides application-level protection and vulnerability management. p. 9

element.name: Agent Orchestrator
element.documentation:
“Agents & Capability – Tools and techniques used to coordinate and execute an autonomous workflow.” and later: “An orchestration layer analyzes incoming requests and routes them to the most appropriate model.” p. 4 and p. 6 (Agent and Capability Layer, LLM Layer)
Sources of information: - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/ai-ml/guide/ai-agent-design-patterns - https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/ai-orchestration - https://masterofcode.com/blog/llm-orchestration - https://www.astronomer.io/blog/introducing-first-gen-ai-cookbook/ - https://www.mtlc.co/cracking-the-code-to-gen-ai-scalability-why-enterprise-orchestration-matters/
Implementations: Specialized products; Airflow / Argo / Kubeflow / Prefect IBM: watsonx Orchestrate Microsoft: Azure AI Foundry, complete platform including orchestration Google: Google Cloud Workflows
Designed to run computational pipelines (extract, train, evaluate, deploy). Each run is usually batch-oriented, immutable, and re-run from scratch if it fails. Optimized for data dependency management, parallel execution, and monitoring compute jobs. Orchestration logic = Python code or YAML DAGs, not BPMN.

element.name: Content Safety & Security Filter
element.documentation: “Mitigation requires a layered security posture encompassing robust input validation, output filtering, continuous monitoring for anomalous activity.” p. 9 (“Security Vulnerability Management”)

element.name: AI Orchestration & Agent Coordination Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide orchestration mechanisms, coordination patterns, and execution control tooling that enable AI Solution owners to design and run multi-agent and multi-step AI workflows, including task routing, hand-offs, dialogue/state management, and tool invocation within controlled platform boundaries.
This capability provides structured orchestration scaffolding (e.g., agent registries, routing rules, workflow definitions, and execution tracing hooks) while leaving substantive accountability for workflow intent, decision logic, and outcome quality with the AI Solution owner.
Purpose - Enable reliable composition of multi-agent and multi-step AI workflows - Reduce complexity of task routing, hand-offs, and state management across agents and tools - Ensure controlled execution within platform boundaries (security, policy, and resource constraints) - Improve traceability and operational clarity of orchestrated AI behavior

element.name: Problem Management
element.documentation: The ability to manage the Lifecycle of all Problems. The primary objectives of Problem Management are to prevent Incidents from happening, and to minimize the Impact of Incidents that cannot be prevented.

element.name: Cloud Compute Environment
element.documentation: “Cloud platforms like Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud allow organizations to deploy models with scalable compute resources.” p. 5 (“Infrastructure and Platform Layers”)

element.name: Retrieval Service
element.documentation: “Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) … enhances large language models by retrieving relevant external documents or data in real time.” p. 6

element.name: LLM Inference Service
element.documentation:
“Large Language Model (LLM) – Complex algorithms and data to learn patterns and structures of language, allowing them to generate human-like text, speech, and images.” p. 4–6
naming: “Inference” clarifies runtime vs training.

element.name: Structurele Scheiding en Isolatie
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Het platform borgt structurele en afdwingbare scheiding tussen tenants, data, contexten en verantwoordelijkheden, onafhankelijk van applicatielogica.
Rationale Isolatie mag niet afhankelijk zijn van applicatiecontroles alleen; structurele scheiding voorkomt escalatie bij fouten of misconfiguratie.
Implicaties - Tenant partitioning: Data, configuratie en workloads zijn logisch en waar nodig fysiek gescheiden per tenant. - Contextisolatie: AI-context (prompts, RAG-data, sessies) wordt strikt gescheiden zodat geen informatie lekt tussen gebruikers of use cases. - Gescheiden credentials: Iedere component of tenant gebruikt eigen identity en secrets. - Minimale privilege-toegang: Toegang is beperkt tot wat strikt noodzakelijk is (least privilege), bij voorkeur via fine-grained policies (RBAC/ABAC). - Netwerk- en runtime-isolatie: Services en workloads zijn gescheiden via netwerksegmentatie en runtime policies. - Sessiescheiding bij AI-interacties: Conversaties en geheugen worden per sessie geïsoleerd opgeslagen.

element.name: Scheiding Data- en Modelinterfaces
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Data-inname, retrieval en opslag worden strikt gescheiden van modelaanroepen of prompt-verwerking.
Rationale Voorkomt dat vertrouwelijke data onbedoeld bij LLM-providers belandt via prompts of logs.
Consequenties Data API (search, graph, ingestion) vs Model API (generation, prompt orchestration). Klant bepaalt of data ooit een extern LLM-endpoint mag bereiken. Security-architectuur moet deze scheiding afdwingen.

element.name: Bias- en Fairness-Monitoring
element.documentation:
Beschrijving AI-systemen moeten gecontroleerd worden op bias, discriminatie en oneerlijke behandeling.
Rationale Verplicht in AI Act en essentieel voor betrouwbare, eerlijke interactie.
Consequenties Periodieke fairness-scans en dashboards. Bij signalen verplicht melden en evalueren. Escalatie- en correctieprocessen vastleggen.

element.name: AI Model Training & Lifecycle Enablement Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide governed data access, compute orchestration, standardized training pipelines, and model artifact lifecycle mechanisms that enable AI Solution owners to (re)train language models or other ML models in a reproducible, controlled, and auditable manner prior to deployment.
This capability provides structured training scaffolding and lifecycle controls (e.g., dataset snapshots, run registration, model registry, approval gates, and packaging hooks) while leaving substantive responsibility for training objectives, data suitability, and model outcomes with the AI Solution owner.
Purpose - Enable controlled and repeatable model (re)training for AI Solutions - Ensure reproducibility through registered configurations, data snapshots, and run lineage - Support secure, governed access to training data and compute resources within agreed boundaries - Reduce risk of uncontrolled training runs, non-traceable model artifacts, and unreviewed model releases

element.name: AI Solution Requirement to Deploy Support
Integrate AI systems with mission-specific rules
AI Platform Product Enablement & Adoption Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to position, communicate, and enable adoption of AI Platform capabilities by professional consumers (such as AI solution developers and business stakeholders), ensuring that platform offerings, constraints, and usage conditions are well understood.
This capability focuses on guiding informed and responsible consumption of platform services rather than commercial sales of AI solutions or end-user products.
What is AI-platform specific: - “Sales” is often internal enablement, not commercial selling - Emphasis on: - reuse of shared knowledge assets - responsible use of AI capabilities - understanding platform constraints and obligations - Supports informed consumption rather than demand generation

element.name: Observability Platform
element.documentation: “Maintaining operational health is crucial. This involves tracking key metrics in real time … Using monitoring tools and automated alerts allows for proactive issue detection.” p. 8 (LLMOps section)

element.name: Sandbox-Experimenteren
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Nieuwe AI-functionaliteit wordt eerst getest in veilige, geïsoleerde omgevingen.
Rationale Biedt innovatie zonder risico voor productie.
Consequenties Isolatie tussen experiment en productie. Vergelijking van modelvarianten. Duidelijke promotiecriteria naar productie.

element.name: Conversation Service
element.documentation:
same passage — provides the means “through which users access and use the LLM.” p. 4
naming: Matches naming in OpenAI/Azure/Anthropic platforms.

element.name: AI Platform Asset Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to identify, register, track, and manage AI Platform assets throughout their lifecycle, ensuring visibility, ownership, and controlled use.
Assets include platform components, knowledge products, configurations, and governance artifacts required for platform operation and accountability.
What is AI-platform specific: - Assets go beyond infrastructure: - datasets and knowledge graphs - model artifacts owned by the platform - policy bundles and evaluation logic - Asset state matters for: - compliance - auditability - reproducibility

element.name: Policy Enforcement & Threat Protection
element.documentation: Provides WAF/IDS/IPS, request inspection, rate-limiting, bot/jailbreak/prompt-injection rules, and egress controls.

element.name: Lifecyclebeheer voor Data & Modellen
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Data, embeddings, indexen en modellen worden volledig beheerd vanaf creatie tot verwijdering.
Rationale Nodig voor consistentie, rollback-mogelijkheden, compliance en evolutie van het platform.
Consequenties Technische retentie- en verwijderregels. Versiebeheer en validatie bij wijzigingen. Governanceproces voor overgang naar archief of verwijdering.

element.name: Prompt Injection Prevention Process
element.documentation: Test and validate prompt templates, embeddings, and orchestration flows to detect and prevent injection or data leakage vulnerabilities.

element.name: Standaardiseer waar mogelijk
element.documentation:
Beschrjiving Standaardiseer waar het kan, maak specifiek waar het moet.
Rationale Standaardisatie reduceert variëteit en kosten, en zorgt voor een betere interoperabiliteit en beveiliging. Hiermee komt bovendien een grotere wendbaarheid tot stand. Door te kiezen voor open standaarden wordt vendor lock-in voorkomen.
Realiseert doel(-en) Kostenefficiënt Uniform Wendbaar
Alle implicaties bij dit architectuurprincipe IMP034 Gebruik de standaard met het meest specifieke werkingsgebied IMP035 Gebruik een actueel register met standaarden IMP038 Gebruik gestandaardiseerde referentiedata IMP075 Gebruik open standaarden voor modellering IMP089 Gebruik standaard oplossingen (implicatie) IMP025 Gebruik standaard oplossingen zonder maatwerk IMP085 Kies bij cloudoplossingen SaaS boven PaaS boven IaaS IMP076 Maak afspraken over nieuwe (en oude) versies van standaarden IMP026 Pas je eigen proces en organisatie aan aan de standaard oplossing IMP036 Pas open standaarden toe IMP037 Pleeg onderhoud op de toegepaste standaarden

element.name: AI Platform Knowledge Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to capture, maintain, and evolve operational and governance knowledge about the AI Platform, including known issues, mitigation patterns, operational insights, and learned risks.
This capability supports continuous improvement, informed oversight, and consistent handling of recurring situations across platform operation and governance.

element.name: AI Platform Service Catalog Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to define, maintain, and publish the service and knowledge product catalog of the AI Platform, providing consumers with a clear, authoritative view of available platform services, knowledge assets, access options, and usage conditions.
The catalog describes what the platform offers, under which constraints, and with what guarantees, independent of any specific AI solution. The catalog primarily targets professional consumers and reflects capability-level offerings rather than end-user services.
What is AI-platform specific: - Catalog includes: - AI services (e.g. retrieval, embedding, evaluation) - knowledge products (datasets, ontologies, prompts) - governance services (approval workflows, audits) - Service descriptions include: - ethical and regulatory constraints - intended usage contexts - lifecycle status (experimental / supported / deprecated)

element.name: Rechtvaardigheid en geen discriminatie
element.documentation:
Beschrijving AI-systemen worden ontworpen en ingezet op een eerlijke, inclusieve en niet-discriminerende wijze.
Rationale Discriminatie is juridisch verboden en ondermijnt vertrouwen in publieke dienstverlening. AI kan onbedoelde bias versterken.
Eerste implicaties - Evaluatie van datasets en modellen op bias - Monitoring van uitkomsten - Correctiemechanismen bij afwijkingen

element.name: Cloud GPU Compute Cluster
element.documentation: “Dedicated hardware (e.g., Nvidia DGX, graphics processing unit [GPU] clusters) provides full control but requires high maintenance.” p. 5 (“Infrastructure and Platform Layers”)

element.name: Observability Service
element.documentation: “Maintaining operational health is crucial. This involves tracking key metrics in real time … monitoring tools and automated alerts allow for proactive issue detection.” p. 8

element.name: Geen Schade en Maatschappelijke Zorgvuldigheid
element.documentation:
Beschrijving AI-systemen worden zorgvuldig ontwikkeld en ingezet zodat risico’s voor individuen, organisaties en samenleving worden geminimaliseerd.
Rationale AI kan onbedoelde negatieve effecten veroorzaken. Zorgvuldigheid en risicobeheersing zijn essentieel voor publieke taakuitvoering.
Eerste implicaties - Risicoanalyse voorafgaand aan inzet - Monitoring van neveneffecten - Escalatie- en correctiemechanismen

element.name: AI Platform Product Development Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to implement, configure, and evolve the software, data, and knowledge components of the AI Platform, including platform services, pipelines, governance controls, and supporting infrastructure, in accordance with approved designs and requirements.
This capability produces deployable platform components and ensures defects are corrected and changes are verified before release.
What is AI-platform specific: - Development includes more than code: - data transformations - ontology and metadata evolution - evaluation logic - policy enforcement logic - Configuration and orchestration are first-class development activities - Strong coupling with architecture and policy constraints

element.name: Layered Policy Enforcement
element.documentation: Beleid wordt afgedwongen op development-time én runtime.

element.name: Veilige Koppelingen
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Alle interne en externe koppelingen moeten beveiligd, geauthenticeerd en geautoriseerd zijn.
Rationale Voorkomt dat gevoelige data uitlekt of gemanipuleerd wordt.
Consequenties Mutual TLS, OAuth2/OIDC, API-tokens verplicht. Beleid voor sleutelbeheer/rotatie. Encryptie in transit en at rest.

element.name: AI Solution Incident & Escalation Support Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to support structured handling and escalation of incidents affecting AI Solutions, including technical failures, governance breaches, unintended outcomes, or harmful behavior detected at runtime.
This capability coordinates with solution owners and governance bodies but does not assume accountability for business decisions.
Purpose - Ensure timely containment of incidents - Provide clear escalation pathways - Support coordinated corrective action - Preserve auditability and traceability

element.name: Resolve Issue
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Resolve Issue`" is to execute the (prepared) course of action to retain, restore, and confirm normal service operations.

element.name: Implement guardrails, such as fact-checking mechanisms and context-aware validations.
AI Platform Request Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to capture, validate, route, and track requests for AI Platform capabilities, access, or changes, ensuring that professional consumer requests are handled consistently, transparently, and in accordance with platform governance rules.
What is AI-platform specific: - Requests may include: - access to sensitive data or knowledge - activation of advanced AI capabilities - exceptions to standard usage - Requests often trigger: - policy checks - oversight or approval workflows - conditional fulfillment paths

element.name: Strategy
element.documentation:
<<<
*Purpose*
The ((Strategy)) functional component manages the creation and maintenance of the portfolio strategy in alignment with the overall business/enterprise strategy.
*Functional Criteria*
The Strategy functional component:
* Must align to business objectives and goals * Shall enable periodic review of the portfolio Strategic Themes * Shall manage the creation and closure of Strategic Objectives as required to address Strategic Themes * Shall provide visibility of both Strategic Themes and initiatives to as broad an audience as possible * Shall ensure traceability of the strategic initiative to the impacted value streams * Must maintain alignment with the Architecture Roadmap(s)
*Data Architecture Criteria*
The Strategy functional component:
* Shall associate one or more Strategic Objectives to a value stream

element.name: Open Standaarden
element.documentation:
Beschrijving Integratie en kennisuitwisseling gebruiken open standaarden (REST, GraphQL, SPARQL, RDF, SKOS/OWL).
Rationale Voorkomt vendor lock-in, bevordert interoperabiliteit en levensduur van integraties.
Consequenties API’s worden contractueel beheerd via API governance. Semantisch vocabulaire wordt met klant/partner afgestemd. Versiebeheer noodzakelijk.

element.name: AI Platform Release Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to plan, coordinate, and control the release of AI Platform components—including services, pipelines, knowledge assets, and governance mechanisms—across environments, ensuring platform integrity, traceability, and controlled transition into operation.
This capability packages and delivers coherent platform releases to fulfillment and operational capabilities while preserving consistency, auditability, and rollback options.
What is AI-platform specific: - Releases may bundle: - code changes - data or knowledge updates - policy or governance changes - Release integrity includes: - reproducibility guarantees - audit trail continuity - alignment between policy, architecture, and runtime behavior

element.name: Model Serving Platform (Hosted LLM)
element.documentation: “The Platform layer includes the software and tools required to develop, train, and deploy AI models.” p. 5 (Infrastructure and Platform Layers)

element.name: Validate token/session
element.documentation:
Token and session validation is enforced at the API Gateway, using identity material and validation capabilities provided by the Authorization Server.
- Validate signature - Validate issuer/audience - Validate expiration - Extract verified claims

element.name: Transparantie en Uitlegbaarheid
element.documentation:
Beschrijving De inzet, werking en beperkingen van AI-systemen zijn inzichtelijk, uitlegbaar en herleidbaar.
Rationale Transparantie is noodzakelijk voor vertrouwen, controleerbaarheid en rechtsbescherming.
Eerste implicaties - Registratie van AI-toepassingen - Logging van besluitvorming - Begrijpelijke communicatie over gebruik van AI

element.name: Platform Governance via Self-Validation
element.documentation: AI-oplossingen valideren zichzelf tegen platformregels; centrale review wordt vervangen door geautomatiseerde admission controls.

element.name: AI Governance & Compliance-by-Design Enablement Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to embed governance controls, documentation requirements, oversight hooks, and policy enforcement mechanisms into the AI Solution realization process, ensuring that compliance obligations are addressed during design and build rather than retroactively.
This capability provides structured governance scaffolding while leaving substantive accountability with the AI Solution owner.
Purpose - Prevent late-stage compliance friction - Ensure auditability and traceability - Align solution implementation with regulatory posture - Reduce risk of uncontrolled deployment

element.name: IT4IT Value Streams
element.documentation:
The IT4IT Value Streams, as shown in <<fig-it4it_value_streams>>, are integrated streams of capabilities within the Architecture Reference Model. They support a holistic lifecycle approach for the planning, creation, delivery, modification, operation, support, and retirement of a Digital Product, combining all of the necessary capabilities to deliver value and support to the dependent parts of the Digital Product lifecycle.
Despite being highly integrated, value creation with regards to Digital Products requires a specific set of capabilities, functions, processes, and data per value stream. In the following sections therefore, for every value stream, the delivered value, main stakeholder(s), a set of scenarios and value stream stages are described in more detail. Value streams interact or depend on other value streams to support the Digital Product throughout the entire lifecycle.

element.name: Operate
element.documentation:
*Overview*
The value stream "`Operate`", as shown in <<fig-operate_value_stream>>, monitors and ensures that the availability and performance of Actual Product Instances are within the boundaries of their agreed Service Contract and Key Performance Indicator (KPI) targets. The scope of this value stream includes managing any compliance and security aspects of running Digital Product Instances and underlying systems that may result from deployment and/or fulfillment. The IT4IT Standard describes how to achieve this using integrated, automated, or fully autonomous data flows between the event monitoring (Event, Incident, Change, Configuration, and Problem Management) functions of the digital services. In addition, the data flows may be designed to be reactive, proactive, or predictive, and include a retrospective.
The Operate value stream delivering the Actual Product Instance is operated in a sustainable way within the agreed terms and conditions of a Service Contract.
*Key Stakeholder*
The primary stakeholder of the Operate value stream is the Consumer, who can use an Actual Product Instance or system within the boundaries of the Service Contract.
*Value*
The outcome of the Operate value stream is to ensure that the Actual Product Instance operates, and can be consumed as agreed in the terms of the Service Contract.
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Operate value stream depends on:
* Deploy: ** Actual Product Instances can be enabled, changed, or disabled by the deployment of the Product Release * Consume: ** Actual Product Instances can be enabled, changed, or disabled by the fulfillment of the Service Offer ** Consumption of a Service Offer can result in an Incident that needs to be handled in the Operate value stream
Value streams that depend on the Operate value stream are:
* Evaluate: ** Improvements start with drivers that are expected to arise from all seven value streams + All value streams will capture data to ensure the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements. Quality is an aspect of each task within each value stream. The Evaluate value stream uses data produced in all value streams to evaluate drivers. * Integrate: ** Problems related to Actual Product Instances can require a fix from development (system defect) * Consume: ** Fulfillment feedback of a Service Offer that resulted in an Incident and needed to be handled in the Operate value stream

element.name: Change Management
element.documentation: The ability to control the lifecycle of all changes from request to resolution. The primary objective of Change Management is to enable beneficial Changes to be made with minimum disruption to Services.

element.name: Entitlement
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The Entitlement data object is a set of attributes that grants or denies access to Service Offers, Digital Product Instances, and associated resources as per the defined Entitlements, such as Entitlement by organization, department, location, group, employee band, user profile, and country.
*Key Attributes*
The Entitlement data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier for every Entitlement type * *Type:* whether the Entitlement can only be viewed or ordered
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Entitlement data object shall maintain the following relationships:
* Entitlement to Service Offer (n:1): provides the Entitlement policy that needs to be attached to the Service Offer for validation * Entitlement to Identity (n:m): indicates what Identities have the given Entitlement

element.name: Deploy
element.documentation:
*Overview*
The value stream "`Deploy`", as shown in <<fig-deploy_value_stream>>, is installing a Product Release into production. It can also imply the removal, disposal, or updating of an existing installed Product Release, so it is made available or unavailable to the customer. Many possible strategies for deploying a Product Release can be scenarios within the Deploy value stream; for example, deployment to a targeted audience, or deployment using a dark launch in which functionality is not directly released to consumers. If the user requirements and/or the characteristics of the Digital Product demand that the installed Product Release is improved, released, and deployed frequently, a drive to automate these value streams can emerge. To comply with regulatory requirements, organizations can require approvals and an audit trail of all the Changes made in production environments. The Deploy value stream ensures that all changes are tracked and related to a specific Product Release.
<<<
The Deploy value stream delivers the initial deployment, update, or removal of an Actual Product Instance, and will put the Service Contract into operation.
*Key Stakeholder*
The main stakeholder of the Deploy value stream is the Product Manager, who can authorize deployment of Actual Product Instances to the customer in the production environment.
*Value*
The outcome of the Deploy value stream is that new Product Releases can be installed safely without causing unexpected outages to the current Actual Product Instances in use.
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Deploy value stream depends on:
* Integrate: ** Provides approved Product Releases that require deployment to the production environment * Consume: ** Delivers a foundation to work on, as an Order can be used to fulfill the underlying setup of a needed production environment
Value streams that depend on the Deploy value stream are:
* Evaluate: ** Improvements start with drivers, that are expected to arise from all seven value streams + All value streams will capture data to ensure that the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements. * Release: ** Deploy a context in the form of an Actual Product Instance in which the Service Offer can be consumed and supported * Operate: ** Actual Product Instances can be enabled, changed, disabled, or removed by the deployment of the Product Release

element.name: Explore
element.documentation:
*Overview*
The value stream "`Explore`", as shown in <<fig-explore_value_stream>>, is performed on the Scope Agreements developed in the Evaluate value stream. It ensures that all planned and accepted Portfolio Backlog Items are further detailed, budgeted, and sourced for Digital Products. This value stream continuously explores new features and/or future directions of a Digital Product aligned with strategic direction and business needs. It ensures that the Product Design evolves to facilitate innovation and optimize business outcomes.
Digital Product Portfolio Backlog Items for investigating a new idea, optimizing existing ideas, or retirement may be resolved by different funding, management, reporting, and contractual obligations for various types of consumers. Differences in budgeting, technology, contracts, business domains, security, or organizational/management structures will influence the portfolios required for an organization.
The Explore value stream is intended to be executed in quick cycles to validate release iterations. The Portfolio Backlog Items should include the generation of a proof-of-concept, conceptual service, or Minimum Viable Product (MVP) with the high-level architectural attributes and activities to begin gathering requirements for the build stage.
The Explore value stream stage ensures that the allocation of resources, budget, and phases for Digital Product releases remain aligned with agreed, planned, and accepted Portfolio Backlog Items and Architecture Blueprints. The primary stakeholder of this value stream is a Product Manager who approves Scope Agreements and release plans for Digital Products.
This value stream can be triggered by a changed Scope Agreement.
*Key Stakeholder*
The primary value stream stakeholder of the Explore value stream is the Product Manager. The Product Manager is responsible for the development and success of a Digital Product for an organization. Product Managers own the business strategy, manage the requirements, and launch features for a product. The Product Manager coordinates the work done by other functions, including: Business Relationship Manager, Architect, Strategist, Technologist, Business Owners, and DevSecOps.
<<<
*Value*
The outcome of the Explore value stream is an accepted and planned Product Backlog with a detailed Scope Agreement, Release Roadmap, and resource allocation. During the Explore stages, Scope Agreements are refined to include product specific details. The value is a Digital Product roadmap with allocated resources.
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Explore value stream depends on:
* Evaluate: ** Delivers qualified, as accepted and planned, Portfolio Backlog Items that will be used to realize the Digital Product and refine the Architecture Blueprint(s) (all product lifecycle stages, scaling and maturing the product, as well as retracting/retirement) and necessary changes to the Enterprise Architecture and Strategic Themes according to the Architecture Roadmap Items * Integrate: ** Provides information on the progress of the Scope Agreement during execution back to the Explore value stream to enable governance on progress and planned dependencies with other Scope Agreements ** If an agreed Scope Agreement cannot be met, the Explore value stream is responsible for changing the Scope Agreement; e.g., change the covered Portfolio Backlog Items (to include more or go for less), change the time or budget, or change the sourcing party
Value streams that depend on the Explore value stream are:
* Evaluate: ** Improvements start with drivers, that are expected to arise from all seven value streams + All value streams will capture data to ensure that the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements * Integrate: ** Will receive an approved Scope Agreement together with an agreed set of Product Backlog Items to start work on a Product Release

element.name: Integrate
element.documentation:
<<< *Overview*
The value stream "`Integrate`", as shown in <<fig-integrate_value_stream>>, creates a new Product Release. It covers the planning, design, development (or configuration), and testing of a new Product Release (in non-production environments), and makes the Product Release available for deployment and release into production. A new Product Release can also be related to an emergency fix needed to resolve a production issue (such as resolving vulnerabilities and critical bugs).
The Integrate value stream covers the creation and modification of all types of Digital Products such as custom-build software, configuration of SaaS applications, Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) software packages, PaaS platforms, Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) products, etc. This value stream is not limited to application development, but also subject to the development and maintenance of infrastructure products (and platforms).
Work is initiated and managed in the Product Backlog (and related team backlogs), which can receive demand signals from consumer or business stakeholders (e.g., new features or business requirements), or from other functional components such as Portfolio Backlog Management, Policy Management, and Problem Management. This may take the form of an approved Portfolio Backlog Item, or may be a smaller-grained signal such as an individual feature, user story, Defect, or Problem.
The Integrate value stream continuously pulls work from the Product Backlog based upon various triggers and inputs; such as:
* New features and requirements raised by a consumer (or a business stakeholder) and/or other product teams * New or modified non-functional requirements such as those related to Service-Level Objectives (SLOs), security, risk, and compliance * New portfolio epics from the portfolio backlog which need to be refined into Product Backlog Items * New versions, updates, or fixes provided by the vendor (e.g., software updates and patches) and/or other technology refresh activities * Problems detected in operational environments (e.g., functional issues, performance issues, etc.) * Emergency changes to resolve major incidents and/or mitigate major risks (e.g., security issues and vulnerabilities)
<<<
The Integrate value stream delivers the Product Release (for a new or modified product) which is packaged for immediate or future deployment through the Deploy value stream.
Note that although the stage is shown as a linear process, the actual work in the value stream consists of multiple iterations, continuous cycles, and flow of work with feedback loops (e.g., Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) and continuous testing, etc.).
*Key Stakeholder(s)*
The typical key stakeholders involved in the Integrate value stream are the Product Manager and associated Development Teams (also referred to as Product Teams, or DevOps Teams if Development and Operations are combined into one team), responsible for the planning, design, creation, and testing of a Product Release.
Other key stakeholders involved are business stakeholders and customer representatives involved in defining business needs (voice of the customer), validating (and testing) the product, and those who ultimately benefit from the value created by using the product.
*Value*
The outcome of the Integrate value stream is a new Product Release, accepted and ready for deployment and release into production. This is a release for a new or existing Digital Product which provides additional value (and/or reduced risk) once made available and used by consumers in production, as a result of:
* New features (e.g., providing additional value by fulfilling new consumer needs) * Non-functional improvements (e.g., improving performance, improve user experience, compliance, security, availability, improved resilience, etc.) * Reducing technology debt (and reducing risks), such as those related to technology upgrades/patches and code quality improvements * Resolving issues/problems (e.g., improve reliability, reduce Incidents in production, improve performance, etc.) * Reduce costs (e.g., improve utilization of resources, etc.)
<<<
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Integrate value stream depends on:
* Explore: ** Delivers Portfolio Backlog Items and an approved Scope Agreement to start creating or updating the Product Backlog ** If the agreed Scope Agreement cannot be met, the Explore value stream is responsible for changing the Scope Agreement within the boundaries of the Architecture Roadmap and Product Portfolio; for example, to allocate more time or a higher budget ** Delivers an updated product vision and high-level roadmap * Consume: ** Deliver the environments to work on, as a request can be used to fulfill the provisioning of a needed development, test, and staging environment * Deploy: ** Deployment of the Product Release into a production environment * Operate: ** Delivers an inflow for Defects, as Problems related to Actual Product Instances may require a fix (or patch) to be created through the Integrate value stream
Value streams that depend on the Integrate value stream are:
* Evaluate: ** The Evaluate value stream uses all lifecycle information from all value streams to identify potential improvements in the product (and/or way of working) * Explore: ** Provides development progress and team capacity + The status of the Product Backlog Item is fed back so it can be taken into account when planning the Portfolio Backlog Items for sourcing. ** If an agreed Scope Agreement cannot be met, the Explore value stream is responsible for changing the Scope Agreement; for example, by changing the covered Portfolio Backlog Items (to include more or go for less), changing the time or budget, or changing the sourcing party * Deploy: ** Provides the approved Product Release, which in turn triggers the deployment scheduling of the Product Release to the production environment * Release: ** Due to a new Product Release, one or more Service Offers need to be modified (or created) to make the new Product Release available for consumption * Operate: ** The Operate value stream depends upon the development of operations features, such as monitoring (and observability), logging, and automated remediation Runbooks + The Integrate value stream needs to develop the required functionality to manage the product in an operational environment.

element.name: Consume
element.documentation:
*Overview*
The value stream "`Consume`", as shown in <<fig-consume_value_stream>>, manages the lifecycle of consuming a Digital Product. Digital Products are consumed as services and the lifecycle is managed as a Subscription to the service.
The ((Consume)) value stream covers the entire set of activities of a consumer, from selecting a Service Offer, ordering the service, fulfillment of the service, getting support, and status on the service.
The consumer can be a person internal to the business or an external customer. The Consumer can also be a system actor that on behalf of an organization consumes the service.
The Consume value stream ensures the ordered Desired Product Instance will be delivered as an Actual Product Instance within the agreed terms. The value stream is responsible for all activities needed to deliver the Actual Product Instance(s) successfully or provide support for them to Consumers.
If ordered, the Consume value stream will create or update the Service Contract. To increase its delivered value and identify areas for improvement, the value stream will also measure the consumption of the Service Contract, and assess the ongoing needs of the stakeholder. These actions support the offering, deployment, and monitoring of the Actual Product Instance, ensuring commitment to the Service Contract.
*Key Stakeholder*
The main stakeholder of the Consume value stream is a customer and/or consumer (human or system actor) in need of a Digital Product.
*Value*
The outcome of the Consume value stream is to have customers able to locate desired Digital Product service offerings, and obtain them in a timely manner as deployed and instantiated Digital Products that will be supported by the Operate value stream, according to a Service Contract.
<<<
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Consume value stream depends on:
* Release: ** Offers are based on releases, and can only be activated after deployment of the release ** The Consume value stream feeds the result of the deployment back into the Release value stream stage for reporting * Operate: ** Fulfillment feedback of a Service Offer that resulted in an Incident and needed to be handled in the Operate value stream
Value streams that depend on the Consume value stream are:
* Evaluate: ** Improvements start with drivers, that are expected to arise from all seven value streams + All value streams will capture data to ensure that the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements. * Integrate: ** A request can be used to fulfill the setup of a test or build environment * Deploy: ** A request can be used to fulfill the underlying platform setup of a needed production environment * Operate: ** Actual Product Instances can be enabled, changed, or disabled by the fulfillment of the Service Offer ** Consumption of a Service Offer can result in an Incident that needs to be handled in the Operate value stream

element.name: Strategic Theme
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Strategic Theme)) data object is a statement of direction and priorities that summarize Strategic Objectives to provide business context for portfolio decision-making.
*Key Attributes*
The Strategic Theme data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier; e.g., number of the Strategic Theme * *Name:* descriptive name of the Strategic Theme * *Description:* description/details of the Strategic Theme
*Key Data Object Relationships*
* Strategic Theme to Strategic Objective (n:m): a Strategic Theme may contribute to the creation of many strategic initiatives; a strategic initiative could be created to address objectives associated to more than one Strategic Theme

element.name: Evaluate
element.documentation:
*Overview*
The value stream "`Evaluate`", as shown in <<fig-evaluate_value_stream>>, contributes to the business strategy and portfolio planning activities. It provides a blueprint for optimizing products, services, and investment Portfolio Management. This value stream is focused on the continuous assessment and evaluation of the entire Digital Product Portfolio to optimize co-creation and alignment of business and technology Strategic Objectives.
Many organizations have portfolio processes and solutions in place but suffer from the following limitations:
* Poor data quality and consistency * No holistic portfolio view across the enterprise * Inconsistent Portfolio, Service, and Product Management * Poor tracking and correlation of the product lifecycle
Organizations need accurate and point-in-time information to understand the inter-relationships and inter-dependencies required to truly orchestrate all the moving parts of Digital Products in ways that can help support business objectives and goals.
The Evaluate value stream provides holistic views of Product Portfolio activities. These views offer an understanding of the inter-relationships among many sub-domains, including Portfolio Management, Enterprise Architecture, Application Management, Information Management, Operations Management, and Information Security Management.
The Evaluate value stream is triggered by a portfolio planning cycle. Portfolio planning can be done in weekly, monthly, or annual cycles.
The overall funding of a Digital Product consists of portfolio epic-based funding (often referred to as Grow the Business (GtB), or discretionary) and product specific investments (often referred to as Run the Business (RtB), or non-discretionary). The product-specific Scope Agreement is defined in the Explore value stream and aligned with the prioritized portfolio level Scope Agreements. This funding methodology enables decentralized decision-making by the Product team.
*Key Stakeholder*
The primary value stream stakeholder of the Evaluate value stream is the Product Portfolio Manager. The Product Portfolio Manager is responsible for ensuring consistent services and portfolio balance through rationalization and sound investments. The Product Portfolio Manager works closely with Strategists, Business Relationship Managers, Architects, and Technologists to align with strategic drivers, consumer demand, and policy standards. They must work together to optimize the portfolio through well-defined control points, data objects, and governance.
<<<
*Value*
The outcome of the Evaluate value stream is a portfolio investment plan with funding and resources to conduct product exploration activities and refine the plan. Here, resources are referred as both IT-related resources such as IT infrastructure, database, software packages and non-IT-related resources such as HR, etc. This value stream delivers an optimized Product Portfolio with secure and quality Digital Products that are consumer friendly and cost effective, accomplished through continuous portfolio improvement and refinement.
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Evaluate value stream depends on:
* All value streams: ** To continually bring in ideas, improvements, and demand to the Evaluate value stream ** To capture data that ensures the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements + Quality is an aspect of each task within each value stream. Quality data may also be used to help analyze the Portfolio Backlog.
Value streams that depend on the Evaluate value stream are:
* All value streams: ** Improvements start with drivers that are expected to arise from all seven value streams + All value streams will capture data to ensure the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements.
* Explore: ** Requires qualified Portfolio Backlog Items that will be used to realize the Product Design, either a new or next Product Design version, and the necessary changes to Enterprise Architecture and Strategic Themes

element.name: Release
element.documentation:
*Overview*
The value stream "`Release`", as shown in <<fig-release_value_stream>>, ensures that the Service Offers for ordering a new, a change in, or the decommissioning of an Actual Product Instance or its support to them are offered to consumers. The Release value stream maintains the Service Offer Catalog, the catalog views, and the Service Offer promotion and information of available Service Offers to the consumer base so that the individual consumer can find and order Digital Products, or support for them. Service Offers should be made available for viewing and requests for access over multiple channels. This value stream publishes the Service Offers based on various triggers and inputs, such as:
* The Product Release, as a result of the Integrate value stream * The decision of the Product Manager to provide a new (bundled) Service Offer based on existing Service Offers and releases * The decision to change or terminate an existing Offer
The Release value stream delivers a digital service by means of a Service Offer that is made available and can be requested.
*Key Stakeholder*
The primary stakeholder of the Release value stream is the Catalog Manager, who is primarily responsible for defining, implementing, and publishing the Service Offers so that they are available to be consumed by the target consumers.
*Value*
The outcome of the Release value stream is to ensure the management of the consumable Service Offers for the Digital Product. These Service Offers are for new service consumption; there are Service Offers for support or to change/retire an Actual Product Instance. This also provides the following additional value to the consumers but is not limited to:
* Centralization of the catalog (e.g., providing access control to all the products and services being offered to the consumers) * Enabling self-service (e.g., the aggregated Service Offer Catalog facilitates user self-service capabilities by providing detailed information about the Service Offer Catalog item and its detailed attributes) * Providing traceability (e.g., the Service Offer Catalog allows traceability of service, from ordering to fulfillment of the service) * Reducing cost (e.g., the Service Offer Catalog reduces the time that the consumer requires from ordering to fulfillment of the service)
*Cross-Value Stream Dependencies*
The Release value stream depends on:
* Deploy: ** Released Offers for the support or consumption of an Actual Product Instances (new, changed, or terminated) can depend on a deployed Product Release
<<<
Value streams that depend on the Release value stream are:
* Evaluate: ** Improvements start with drivers, that are expected to arise from all seven value streams + All Value Streams will capture data to ensure that the quality of the Digital Product will meet the requirements. * Consume: ** Service Offers that can be fulfilled are based on released Service Offers, and can only be activated after deployment of the release ** The Consume value stream feeds the result of the deployment back into the Release value stream stages for reporting

element.name: Resource
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Resource)) data object represents an asset or other entity that is limited in nature. It can be allocated to a Digital Product or it can be unallocated.
*Key Attributes*
* *Id:* unique identifier for each Resource * *Type:* describes the type of each Resource pool * *Status:* record if the Resource is available
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Resource data object shall maintain the following relationships:
* Resource to Desired Product Instance (n:1): indicates if a Resource is allocated to a given Product Instance * Resource to Usage Record (1:n): provides Resource usage details for cost computation

element.name: Network Connectivity Service
element.documentation: “Underlying physical and virtual resources required to support the model (e.g., hardware, storage solutions, networking resources).” p. 4 (GenAI Tech Stack diagram description)

element.name: AI Platform Security Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to define, implement, operate, and continuously improve platform-wide security controls across infrastructure, platform software, integrations, data handling, and operational processes.
This capability ensures that security is treated as a managed cross-cutting concern, addressing confidentiality, integrity, and protection of platform assets without reducing security to only compliance or access management.
Purpose - Ensure platform-wide implementation of security controls - Protect data, services, integrations, and platform components against threats and misuse - Reduce fragmentation of security responsibilities across capabilities - Support secure operation of the AI Platform as a shared foundation for multiple AI Solutions

element.name: AI Platform Product Backlog Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to manage and prioritize the delivery backlog of the AI Platform, covering enhancements to platform services, knowledge assets, governance capabilities, architecture remediation, and non-functional improvements required to evolve the platform in a controlled manner.
This capability structures and sequences platform work items derived from requirements, defects, architectural decisions, and policy changes, without managing backlogs of downstream AI solutions.
What is AI-platform specific: - Backlog items often originate from: - regulatory interpretation changes - architectural evolution (e.g. new evaluation or oversight capabilities) - learnings from platform operation (feedback loops)
Backlog balances: - innovation vs. control - scalability vs. explainability - performance vs. governance depth

element.name: AI Platform Portfolio Backlog Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to manage, prioritize, and provide transparency into the portfolio backlog of the AI Platform, covering platform enhancements, new or changed platform services, knowledge assets, governance mechanisms, and non-functional improvements.
This capability ensures that evolution of the AI Platform is aligned with enterprise strategy, demand signals, available capacity, and governance constraints, without managing or prioritizing the internal backlogs of individual AI solutions.

element.name: Implement Service Offer
element.documentation:
*Description*
The purpose of the value stream stage "`Implement Service Offer`" is to onboard the Service Offer into the Service Offer Catalog and to prepare all that is needed so that the Service Offer can be published.

element.name: Open en Interoperabel Ontwerp
element.documentation: Het platform gebruikt open standaarden voor integratie en kennisrepresentatie.

element.name: AI Platform Continuity Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to ensure the continuity and recoverability of AI Platform services, knowledge assets, and governance functions in the event of disruptions, failures, or loss of critical components.
Continuity planning addresses not only technical recovery but also preservation of auditability, traceability, and trust in platform behavior after recovery.

element.name: Compliance Management
element.documentation:
The Governance, Risk, & Compliance function ensures the transparency and traceability of the risks and compliance information related to the Digital Product delivery. It is tightly linked to the Policy functional component where the Policies and related control requirements are maintained.
Managing risk and compliance is an integral part of the development and delivery of Digital Products. The Governance, Risk, & Compliance function ensures that security, risk, and compliance are fully embedded into the digital delivery network, including all involved parties and vendors.

element.name: Model Serving Service (Cloud)
element.documentation:
same passage — offers the deployment capability. p. 5
Generic LLM Execution Service

element.name: Knowledge Item
element.documentation:
*Purpose*
The ((Knowledge Item)) data object contains information to be used by both operators and the consuming business as structured searchable content managed and maintained.
*Key Attributes*
The Knowledge Item data object shall have the following key data attributes:
* *Id:* unique identifier of the Knowledge Item * *Title:* title of the Knowledge Item * *Status:* for example, draft, revise, published, review, archived * *Author Id:* unique identifier of the responsible author * *Publish Date:* date/time on which the item is available for publication * *Expiry Date:* date/time on which the item is no longer visible * *Body:* body text, video, or any other content that is published
*Key Data Object Relationships*
The Knowledge Item data object shall maintain the following relationships:
* Knowledge Item to Problem (n:m): if a link to a Problem exists, the Knowledge Item will need changing when a Problem is (partly) resolved * Knowledge Item to Interaction (n:m): one or more Knowledge Items can be related to an Interaction when a consumer searches for knowledge during a self service experience * Knowledge Item to Incident (n:m): one or more Knowledge Items can be related to an Incident as part of the Incident resolution * Knowledge Item to Knowledge Item (n:m): knowledge can be linked if related

element.name: AI Configuration & Build Support Capability
element.documentation:
The ability of the AI Platform to provide tooling, configuration mechanisms, pipelines, and development interfaces that enable AI Solution owners to configure goals, knowledge, rules, policies, and evaluation settings and to compose solution workflows/pipelines (e.g., pre/post-processing steps such as translation, redaction, formatting) within controlled platform boundaries.
This capability enables structured implementation without exposing underlying platform internals.
Purpose - Support controlled solution realization - Prevent ad-hoc or unsupported configuration patterns - Ensure traceability of configuration changes - Preserve platform integrity

element.name: Standaardplatform met configureerbare bouwblokken, maatwerk alleen aan de rand
AI Platform Financial Management Capability
element.documentation:
The ability to plan, monitor, and manage the financial aspects of the AI Platform, providing transparency into platform costs, investments, and operational expenditures, including the cost of compute, data, governance, and compliance.
This capability supports sustainable platform operation and informed decision-making on capacity, prioritization, and platform evolution.
